comics.altText: Sure, this exoplanet we discovered may seem hostile to life, but our calculations suggest it's actually in the accretion disc's habitable zone.

comics.title: Reading a Big Number
comics.altText: [desperately] Maybe this is from some country where they use commas as decimal points, and also as digit separators after the decimal, and also use random other characters for decoration???

comics.title: Good Science
comics.altText: If you think curiosity without rigor is bad, you should see rigor without curiosity.

comics.title: Alert Sound
comics.altText: With a good battery, the device can easily last for 5 or 10 years, although the walls probably won't.

comics.title: Neighbor-Source Heat Pump
comics.altText: The installation of the pipes on the inside of the insulation can be challenging, especially when the neighbor could come home at any minute.

comics.title: Trojan Horse
comics.altText: Ultimately, history would imperfectly record the story of the Foal of Troy.

comics.title: Bridge Types
comics.altText: Pontoon bridges are just linear open-sided waterbeds.

comics.title: Check Engine
comics.altText: They say it's probably safe to keep orbiting for a while, but if it stays on or starts flashing we might have to call someone.

comics.title: Archaea
comics.altText: Under the two-domain system, anyone who punches you is technically an Archaean pathogen.

comics.title: Mass Spec
comics.altText: Patients at least found it to be an improvement over Millikan's incredibly messy and unpleasant oil drop suspension procedure.

comics.title: Drafting
comics.altText: A 5% efficiency gain at the cost of a 99% efficiency loss

comics.title: Baker's Units
comics.altText: 169 is a baker's gross.

comics.title: Renormalization
comics.altText: Applying renormalization to bullies successfully transformed Pete & Pete's Endless Mike into Finite Mike.

comics.title: Sail Physics
comics.altText: Turning in other directions can be accomplished by using a magnetized centerboard and ocean currents, since a current flowing through a magnetic field induces a Laplace force.

comics.title: Modern
comics.altText: Scholars are still debating whether the current period is post-postmodern or neo-contemporary.

comics.title: Deposition
comics.altText: P.S. If you have time travel, come to my birthday party Saturday!

comics.title: Pascal's Law
comics.altText: Reductio ad absurdum fails when reality is absurd.

comics.title: Globe Safety
comics.altText: Frankly, given their extreme gravitational fields and general instability, even 12-inch globes should probably be banned.

comics.title: About 20 Pounds
comics.altText: In addition to gravity, burritos interact through the strong, weak, and electromagnetic forces, which is believed to be a major contributor to their popularity.

comics.title: Unstoppable Force and Immovable Object
comics.altText: Unstoppable force-carrying particles can't interact with immovable matter by definition.

comics.title: Jupiter Core
comics.altText: Juno mission data suggests that Jupiter actually contains Matryoshka doll-style nested copies of every other planet in the Solar System.

comics.title: Chess Position
comics.altText: It's important to learn the moves that take you into the vortex, but it's best not to study vortex itself too closely. Even grandmasters who have built up a tolerance lose the ability to play for a few hours after studying it.

comics.title: PhD Timeline
comics.altText: Rümeysa Öztürk was grabbed off the street in my town one month ago.

comics.title: Tennis Balls
comics.altText: After initial tests created a series of large holes in the wall of the lab, the higher-power Scanning Tunneling Tennis Ball Microscope project was quickly shut down.

comics.title: Air Fact
comics.altText: 'Wow, that must be why you swallow so many of them per year!' 'No, that's spiders. You swallow WAY more ants.'

comics.title: Anchor Bolts
comics.altText: The biggest expense was installing the mantle ducts to keep the carbonate-silicate cycle operating.

comics.title: de Sitter
comics.altText: Our anti-de Sitter club is small at the moment, but I've started corresponding with the conformal field theory people.

comics.title: The Roads Both Taken
comics.altText: When you worry that you're missing out on something by not making both choices simultaneously by quantum superposition, that's called phomo.

comics.title: Anachronym Challenge
comics.altText: I have to pay with paper money.

comics.title: Push Notifications
comics.altText: NOTIFICATION: Now dismissing a head of the Notification Hydra… NOTIFICATION: Success! You have dismissed a head of the Notification Hydra!

comics.title: Tariffs
comics.altText: [later] I don't get why our pizza slices have such terrible reviews; the geotextile-infused sauce gives the toppings incredible slope stability!

comics.title: Stargazing 4
comics.altText: We haven't actually seen a star fall in since we invented telescopes, but I have a list of ones I'm really hoping are next.

comics.title: Decay Chain
comics.altText: If you have an old phone in a drawer, and you listen very carefully, you can occasionally hear the occasional tap of an emitted SIM card hitting the side of the drawer as the phone transmutes to a lower-end model.

comics.title: Orogeny
comics.altText: Most properties can only boast INDOOR heated floors.

comics.title: Terror Bird
comics.altText: There has long been debate about Big Bird's species, with some experts claiming he was a canary, but recent genetic analysis places him firmly in Cariamiformes.

comics.title: Rock Identification
comics.altText: 'Is it worth anything?' 'I dunno, is the answer to that question worth another $5?'

comics.title: SawStart
comics.altText: Unfortunately, SawStart is one-use-only. Once started, the blade cannot be stopped, and must be replaced with a fresh blade while the running one is carefully disposed of.

comics.title: Cosmic Distance Calibration
comics.altText: This is the biggest breakthrough since astronomers noticed that the little crosshairs around red giant stars starting to burn helium are all the same size.

comics.title: Square Units
comics.altText: The biggest I've seen in a published source in the wild is an 80-fold error in a reported distance, which I think came from a series of at least three unit conversions and area/length misinterpretations.

comics.title: Lungfish
comics.altText: I know having so many base pairs makes rebasing complicated, but you're in Bilateria, so shouldn't you at LEAST be better at using git head?

comics.title: Planet Definitions
comics.altText: Under the 'has cleared its orbital neighborhood' and 'fuses hydrogen into helium' definitions, thanks to human activities Earth technically no longer qualifies as a planet but DOES count as a star.

comics.title: Off By One
comics.altText: It does come at the small cost of a LOT more off-by-40-or-50 errors.

comics.title: Water Balloons
comics.altText: Update: The physics department has recruited an astronomer who studies meteor fireballs.

comics.title: Omniroll
comics.altText: It seems wrong that Fruit by the Foot is only sold by weight or by number of rolls.

comics.title: Water Damage
comics.altText: Your homeowner's insurance might cover it, but be sure to check the subductible.

comics.title: Tall Structures
comics.altText: Briefly set a new record for tallest human-made structure by getting my knit sweater snagged on the skydiving plane door as I jumped and not noticing until I'd landed.

comics.title: Excusing Yourself
comics.altText: The most awkward part is when you have to pause to put on your shoes before you continue rolling out the door.

comics.title: RNA
comics.altText: 2040s: RNA formed the basis for life each of the five known times it arose on the early Earth.

comics.title: Giants
comics.altText: I can't get over the suspicion that all those viral pictures are photoshopped and 'Flemish' belongs in the lower right circle.

comics.title: Scream Cipher
comics.altText: AAAAAA A ÃA̧AȂA̦ ǍÅÂÃĀÁȂ AAAAAAA!

comics.title: KM3NeT
comics.altText: Unfortunately, KM3NeT led to the discovery of the Pauli anglerfish, which emits Cherenkov radiation to prey on neutrino researchers.

comics.title: Archive Request
comics.altText: They just want researchers in the enclosure to feel enriched and stimulated. ('The Enclosure' is what archivists call the shadowy world outside their archives in which so many people are trapped.)

comics.title: Hardwood
comics.altText: They may technically have been softwoods at some point, but they definitely aren't now.

comics.title: Atom
comics.altText: What's weirder is that muons turned out to be INCREDIBLY cute.

comics.title: Incoming Asteroid
comics.altText: The bottom ones are also potentially bad news for any other planets in our solar system that have been counting on Earth having a stable orbit.

comics.title: Suspension Bridge
comics.altText: As a first step, they can put in a secondary deck, to help drivers try it out and find out how fun the jumps are. After a while no one will use the old flat deck and they can remove it.

comics.title: Rotary Tool
comics.altText: It was great until my thumb slipped and I accidentally launched my telescope into the air at Mach 8.

comics.title: Stromatolites
comics.altText: If only my ancestors had been fortunate enough to marry into the branch of the bacteria family that could photosynthesize, like all my little green cousins here.

comics.title: AlphaMove
comics.altText: It struggles a little with complex positions, like when there are an even number of moves and it has to round down, but when run against itself it's capable of finding some novelties. At one point I saw six knights on the board at once; Stockfish rarely exceeds four.

comics.title: Humidifier Review
comics.altText: They should add a little sticker that certifies that the humidifier supports water conservation, but in the sense of energy conservation or momentum conservation.

comics.title: Muons
comics.altText: Update: I've been banned from the physics department for the way I pronounce "Doppler effect."

comics.title: T. Rex Evolution
comics.altText: Unfortunately, body size and bite force continue to increase.

comics.title: Unit Circle
comics.altText: They're continuing to search for a square with the same area as the circle, as efforts to construct one have run into difficulties.

comics.title: Chemical Formulas
comics.altText: Can you pass the nackle?

comics.title: Human Altitude
comics.altText: I wonder what surviving human held the record before balloons (excluding edge cases like jumping gaps on a mountain bridge). Probably it was someone falling from a cliff into snow or water, but maybe it involved something weird like a gunpowder explosion or volcano.

comics.title: Uncanceled Units
comics.altText: Speed limit c arcminutes^2 per steradian

comics.title: Radon
comics.altText: A good ²³⁸Umbrella policy should cover it.

comics.title: Chess Zoo
comics.altText: The zoo takes special care to keep kings separated from opposite-color pieces as part of their conservation program to prevent mating in captivity.

comics.title: Trimix
comics.altText: You don't want the nitrogen percentage to be too high or you run the risk of eutrophication.

comics.title: Features of Adulthood
comics.altText: I don't dig pit traps and cover them with sticks and a thin layer of leaves nearly as much as I expected; I find a chance to do it barely once a month.

comics.title: Origami Black Hole
comics.altText: You may notice the first half of these instructions are similar to the instructions for a working nuclear fusion device. After the first few dozen steps, be sure to press down firmly and fold quickly to overcome fusion pressure.

comics.title: Skew-T Log-P
comics.altText: The most important quantity for meteorologists is of course the product of latent pressure and temperostrophic enthalpy, though 'how nice the weather is' is a close second.

comics.title: Time Capsule Instructions
comics.altText: Inside is a third box, labeled DO NOT OPEN UNLESS YOU ARE IN THE TIME ZONE WHERE YOU OPENED BOTH PREVIOUS BOXES.

comics.title: Lasering Incidents
comics.altText: I still don't know how the police found my compound where I ran an illegal searchlight depot/covert blimp airfield/fireworks testing range.

comics.title: Sun Avoidance
comics.altText: C'mon, ESA Solar Orbiter team, just give the Parker probe a LITTLE nudge at aphelion. Crash it into the sun. Fulfill the dream of Icarus. It is your destiny.

comics.title: D Roll
comics.altText: Under some circumstances, if you throw a D8 and then a D12 at an enemy, thanks to the D8's greater pointiness you actually have to roll a D12 and D8 respectively to determine damage.

comics.title: Exclusion Principle
comics.altText: Fermions are weird about each other in a standoffish way. Integer-spin particles are weird about each other in a 'stand uncomfortably close while talking' kind of way.

comics.title: Linear Sort
comics.altText: The best case is O(n), and the worst case is that someone checks why.

comics.title: Phase Change
comics.altText: Even when you try to make nice, smooth ice cubes in a freezer, sometimes one of them will shoot out a random ice spike, which physicists ascribe to kiki conservation.

comics.title: METAR
comics.altText: In the aviation world, they don't use AM/PM times. Instead, all times are assumed to be AM unless they're labeled NOTAM.

comics.title: The Maritime Approximation
comics.altText: It works because a nautical mile is based on a degree of latitude, and the Earth (e) is a circle.

comics.title: Making Tea
comics.altText: No, of course we don't microwave the mug WITH the teabag in it. We microwave the teabag separately.

comics.title: Seismologists
comics.altText: And even when they're not distracted, they usually get kicked out for illegal under-the-net 'subduction spikes'.

comics.title: Infinite Armada Chess
comics.altText: Stockfish 16 suggests the unconventional opening 1. RuntimeError: Out of bounds memory access

comics.title: Advent Calendar Advent Calendar
comics.altText: The growth rate of items per day may may seem absurd, but it's actually much less than the acceleration in the 12 Days of Christmas song.

comics.title: Second Stage
comics.altText: Hmm, they won't do in-flight delivery, so let's order a new first and second stage to our emergency landing site and then try to touch down on top of them to save time.

comics.title: Neutrino Modem
comics.altText: Our sysadmin accidentally won a Nobel Prize while trying to debug neutrino oscillation error correction.

comics.title: Cold Air
comics.altText: We also should really have checked that the old water tower was disconnected from the water system before we started filling it with compressed air.

comics.title: D Combinatorics
comics.altText: Look, you can't complain about this after giving us so many scenarios involving N locked chests and M unlabeled keys.

comics.title: Arizona Chess
comics.altText: Sometimes, you have to sacrifice pieces to gain the advantage. Sometimes, to advance ... you have to fall back.

comics.title: Kedging Cannon
comics.altText: The real key was inventing the windmill-powered winch.

comics.title: The Future of Orion
comics.altText: Dinosaur Cosmics

comics.title: Europa Clipper
comics.altText: They had BETTER make this a sample return mission.

comics.title: Geometriphylogenetics
comics.altText: There's a maximum likelihood that I'm doing phylogenetics wrong.

comics.title: Number Shortage
comics.altText: "10 minutes ago we were down to only 2 0s!" "How many do we have now?" "I ... don't know!!"

comics.title: Proterozoic Rocks
comics.altText: These rocks are from a time before eyes, brains, and bones, pieces of a land warmed by an unseen sun.

comics.title: Probabilistic Uncertainty
comics.altText: "One popular strategy is to enter an emotional spiral. Could that be the right approach? We contacted several researchers who are experts in emotional spirals to ask them, but none of them were in a state to speak with us."

comics.title: Demons
comics.altText: Though they do appreciate how much he improved the heating system for the flame pit.

comics.title: Disposal
comics.altText: We were disappointed that the rocket didn't make a THOOOONK noise when it went into the tube, but we're setting up big loudspeakers for future launches to add the sound effect.

comics.title: Wells
comics.altText: You do have to be careful, though--sometimes, instead of water, you hit this free fuel that you can sell for a lot of money instead.

comics.title: Sandwich Helix
comics.altText: The number one rule of string manipulation is that youâ€™ve got to specify your encodings.

comics.title: RNAWorld
comics.altText: Disney lore: Canonically, because of how Elsa's abiogenesis powers work, Olaf is an RNA-only organism.

comics.title: Temperature Scales
comics.altText: In my new scale, °X, 0 is Earths' record lowest surface temperature, 50 is the global average, and 100 is the record highest, with a linear scale between each point and adjustment every year as needed.

comics.title: Experimental Astrophysics
comics.altText: Our experiment will be expensive, but we believe it will produce important spin-offs, especially if we manage to hit the sun from the right angle.

comics.title: Bad Map Projection: The United Stralia
comics.altText: This projection distorts both area and direction, but preserves Melbourne.

comics.title: Ravioli-Shaped Objects
comics.altText: It's a real accomplishment to mess up a ravioli recipe badly enough that the resulting incident touches all four quadrants of the NFPA hazard diamond.

comics.title: Solar Protons
comics.altText: If any of you want to meet some cool local oxygen atoms, I can introduce you!

comics.title: CIDABM
comics.altText: There's a heated debate over whether the big island of Tierra del Fuego should qualify for membership.

comics.title: University Commas
comics.altText: The distinctive 'UCLA comma' and 'Michigan comma' are a long string of commas at the start and end of the sentence respectively.

comics.title: Númenor Margaritaville
comics.altText: I see white shores, and beyond it, a far green country under a tequila sunrise.

comics.title: Ingredients
comics.altText: Add main-belt asteroids to taste.

comics.title: UK Coal
comics.altText: The Watership Down rabbits removed an additional 0.1 nanometers constructing their warren, although that was mostly soil. British rabbits have historically mined very little coal; the sole rabbit-run coal plant was shut down in the 1990s.

comics.title: Beamsplitters
comics.altText: Under quantum tax law, photons sent through a beamsplitter don't actually choose which path they took, or incur a tax burden, until their wavefunction collapses when the power is sold.

comics.title: Late Cenozoic
comics.altText: Our nucleic acid recovery techinques found a great deal of homo sapiens DNA incorporated into the fossils, particularly the ones containing high levels of resin, leading to the theory that these dinosaurs preyed on the once-dominant primates.

comics.title: Physics Lab Thermostat
comics.altText: Hopefully the HVAC people set it to only affect the AIR in the room.

comics.title: Maslow's Pyramid
comics.altText: The local police, building inspector, and fire marshal are all contesting my 'safety' assertion, or would be if they could reach me past all the traps.

comics.title: Tectonic Surfing
comics.altText: The worst is when you wipe out in the barrel and you're trapped for several million years until erosion frees you.

comics.title: Every Scientific Field
comics.altText: Conveniently for everyone, it turns out that dark energy is produced by subterranean parasitoid wasps.

comics.title: Craters
comics.altText: It's annoying that the Nastapoka Arc isn't a meteor impact crater, but I truly believe that--with enough time, effort, and determination--we could make it one.

comics.title: Asteroid News
comics.altText: Their calculations show it will 'pass within the distance of the moon' but that it 'will not hit the moon, so what's the point?'

comics.title: Monocaster
comics.altText: My competitors say the tiny single tiny caster is unsafe, unstable, and offers no advantages over traditional designs, to which I say: wow, why are you guys so mean? I thought we were friends!

comics.title: Water Filtration
comics.altText: You'd think the most expensive part would be the quark-gluon plasma chamber, but it's actually usually the tube to the top of the atmosphere to carry the cosmic rays down.

comics.title: Slingshots
comics.altText: In my reboot, Dennis the Menace was just trying to send Mr. Wilson a nice comet, but accidentally wiped out his dinosaur garden.

comics.title: Lava Lakes
comics.altText: Hey, golf balls float on lava, so this should make recovering them from the hazards easier.

comics.title: Sky Alarm
comics.altText: During the day it also activates for neat clouds and pretty sunsets.

comics.title: Stranded
comics.altText: At least they're not alone down there.

comics.title: Three Kinds of Research
comics.altText: The secret fourth kind is 'we applied a standard theory to their map of every tree and got some suspicious results.'

comics.title: Time Traveler Causes of Death
comics.altText: Many a hungry time traveler has Googled 'trilobites shellfish allergy' only to find their carrier had no coverage in the Ordovician.

comics.title: Classical Periodic Table
comics.altText: Personally I think mercury is more of a 'wet earth' hybrid element.

comics.title: Storage Tanks
comics.altText: We're considering installing a pressurization system to keep the tanks at constant pressure solely to deter them.

comics.title: Ferris Wheels
comics.altText: They left the belt drive in place but switched which wheel was powered, so people could choose between a regular ride, a long ride, and a REALLY long ride.

comics.title: Helium Synthesis
comics.altText: Our lawyers were worried because it turns out the company inherits its debt from the parent universe, but luckily cosmic inflation reduced it to nearly zero.

comics.title: Celestial Event
comics.altText: If we can get a brood of 13-year cicadas going, we might have a chance at making this happen before the oceans evaporate under the expanding sun.

comics.title: Meteor Shower PSA
comics.altText: If you hold the meteor too long, it may imprint on you and form a contact binary, making reintroduction to space difficult.

comics.title: Vice President First Names
comics.altText: [Political pundit on the ScrabbleTV News channel] "After four years of defying orthographic pressure, Joe ceded the top of the ticket to Kamala, who--after considering Josh, Mark, Andy, Roy, and Pete--picked Tim."

comics.title: University Age
comics.altText: This only makes it more urgent that we adopt my roadmap for the next 10 years, which should put us solidly in the lead.

comics.title: Matter
comics.altText: He was the first person to land a 900, which is especially impressive because pulling off a half-integer spin requires obeying Fermi-Dirac statistics.

comics.title: Exam Numbers
comics.altText: Calligraphy exam: Write down the number 37, spelled out, nicely.

comics.title: Chili Tornado Quake
comics.altText: Buildings constructed from softer materials were damaged by chili pepper impacts to the storm's high Richter-Fujita-Scoville-Mohs hardness rating.

comics.title: Olympic Sports
comics.altText: Thankfully for everyone involved, the Winter Olympics officials spotted me and managed to stop me before I got to the ski jump.

comics.title: House Inputs and Outputs
comics.altText: People think power over ethernet is so great, and yet when I try to do water over ethernet everyone yells at me.

comics.title: President Venn Diagram
comics.altText: Hard to imagine political rhetoric more microtargeted at me than 'I love Venn diagrams. I really do, I love Venn diagrams. It's just something about those three circles.'

comics.title: CrowdStrike
comics.altText: We were going to try swordfighting, but all my compiling is on hold.

comics.title: Organ Meanings
comics.altText: IMO the thymus is one of the coolest organs and we should really use it in metaphors more.

comics.title: Beam of Light
comics.altText: Einstein's theories solved a longstanding mystery about Mercury: Why it gets so hot. "It's because," he pointed out, "the sun is right there."

comics.title: Hatchery
comics.altText: Anadromous fish are more vulnerable in rivers, since the lack of salt means you can quickly crack passwords using rainbow trout tables.

comics.title: A Crossword Puzzle
comics.altText: Hint: If you ever encounter this puzzle in a crossword app, just [term for someone with a competitive and high-achieving personality].

comics.title: Number Line Branch
comics.altText: Attention all passengers: This is an express sequence to infinity. If your stop is not a power of two, please disembark now.

comics.title: Pole Vault
comics.altText: Ugh, and we JUST went through this yesterday with javelin.

comics.title: Bracket Symbols
comics.altText: ’"‘”’" means "I edited this text on both my phone and my laptop before sending it"

comics.title: Alien Theories
comics.altText: They originally came here to try to investigate our chemtrail technology, and got increasingly frustrated when all their samples turned out to just be water ice with trace amounts of jet exhaust.

comics.title: Routine Maintenance
comics.altText: The worst was the time they accidentally held the can upside down and froze all the Earth's magma chambers solid.

comics.title: Bad Map Projection: Exterior Kansas
comics.altText: Although Kansas is widely thought to contain the geographic center of the contiguous 48 states, topologists now believe that it's actually their outer edge.

comics.title: Situation
comics.altText: We're right under the flight path for the scheduled orbital launch, but don't worry--it's too cold out for the rockets to operate safely, so I'm sure they'll postpone.

comics.title: Network Configuration
comics.altText: If you repeatedly rerun the development of technological civilization, it turns out that for some reason the only constant is that there is always a networking utility called 'netcat', though it does a different thing in each one.

comics.title: Electric vs Gas
comics.altText: An idling gas engine may be annoyingly loud, but that's the price you pay for having WAY less torque available at a standstill.

comics.title: Pascal's Wager Triangle
comics.altText: In contrast to Pascal's Wager Triangle, Pascal's Triangle Wager argues that maybe God wants you to draw a triangle of numbers where each one is the sum of the two numbers above it, so you probably should, just in case.

comics.title: 1.2 Kilofives
comics.altText: 'Oh yeah? Give me 50 milliscore reasons why I should stop.'

comics.title: Broken Model
comics.altText: In addition to eating foxes, rabbits can eat grass. The grass also eats foxes. Our equations chart the contours of Fox Hell.

comics.title: Magnet Fishing
comics.altText: The ten-way tie was judged a ten-way tie, so no one won the grand prize, a rare fishing monopole.

comics.title: Unsolved Chemistry Problems
comics.altText: I'm an H⁺ denier, in that I refuse to consider loose protons to be real hydrogen, so I personally believe it stands for 'pretend'.

comics.title: Fluid Speech
comics.altText: Thank you to linguist Gretchen McCulloch for teaching me about phonetic assimilation, and for teaching me that if you stand around in public reading texts from a linguist and murmuring example phrases to yourself, people will eventually ask if you're okay.

comics.title: Cell Organelles
comics.altText: It's believed that Golgi was originally an independent organism who was eventually absorbed into our cells, where he began work on his Apparatus.

comics.title: Modes of Transportation
comics.altText: My bold criticism might anger the hot air balloon people, which would be a real concern if any of them lived along a very narrow line directly upwind of me.

comics.title: Complexity Analysis
comics.altText: PERPETUALLY OPTIMISTIC CASE: Early in the execution, our research group makes a breakthrough on proving P=NP.

comics.title: Local Group
comics.altText: Cosmologists estimate the spaghetti strand to be about 200 septillion calories, though it could be higher depending on the nutritional value of dark matter.

comics.title: Room Code
comics.altText: Sorry to make you memorize this random string of digits. If it helps, it can also double as a mnemonic for remembering your young relatives' birthdays, if they happened to have been born on February 5th, 2018.

comics.title: Exponential Growth
comics.altText: Karpov's construction of a series of increasingly large rice cookers led to a protracted deadlock, but exponential growth won in the end.

comics.title: Ocean Loop
comics.altText: I can't believe they wouldn't even let me hold a vote among the passengers about whether to try the loop.

comics.title: Bloom Filter
comics.altText: Sometimes, you can tell Bloom filters are the wrong tool for the job, but when they're the right one you can never be sure.

comics.title: Elementary Physics Paths
comics.altText: ==COSMOLOGY==> 'Uhhh ... how sure are we that everything is made of these?'

comics.title: Driving PSA
comics.altText: This PSA brought to you by several would-be assassins who tried to wave me in front of speeding cars in the last month and who will have to try harder next time.

comics.title: Chasing
comics.altText: Certain hybrid events can only happen in certain locations where all the conditions are present; chasers flock to the area in and around Kansas known as tumbleweed-colliding-with-possum alley.

comics.title: Google Solar Cycle
comics.altText: From Google Trends, it looks like the lag between people Googling cocktail recipes and 'hangover cure' is 14 hours.

comics.title: Good and Bad Ideas
comics.altText: While it seemed like a fun prank at the time, I realize my prank fire extinguishers full of leaded gasoline were a mistake.

comics.title: Software Testing Day
comics.altText: The company tried to document how often employees were celebrating Software Testing Day, but their recordkeeping system kept mysteriously crashing.

comics.title: Alphabetical Cartogram
comics.altText: Poor Weeoming.

comics.title: Doppler Effect

comics.title: DateTime

comics.title: Deviled Eggs
comics.altText: The foil on the toothpick represents the blue flash.

comics.title: False Dichotomy
comics.altText: There are two types of dichotomy: False dichotomies, true dichotomies, and surprise trichotomies.

comics.title: Qua
comics.altText: Qua qua qua is the sine qua non of sine qua non qua sine qua non.

comics.title: I Shouldn't Complain
comics.altText: Bald-faced hornets are only a 2 on the Schmidt pain index, so I shouldn't complain. The tennis ball ejected from the dryer exhaust vent could have ricocheted off the nest of a much higher-scoring insect before knocking me off the ladder. Really, I'm lucky.

comics.title: Outlet Denier
comics.altText: There are regularly placed bumps on the underside just the right size to press the rocker switch on the power strip.

comics.title: Party Quadrants
comics.altText: Single-elimination might provide more drama, but I think we can all agree that a comprehensive numerical scoring system will let us better judge the party's winner.

comics.title: For the Sake of Simplicity
comics.altText: For the sake of simplicity, gardeners are assumed to move through Euclidean space--neglecting the distortion from general relativity--unless they are in the vicinity of a Schwarzschild Orchid.

comics.title: Greek Letters
comics.altText: If you ever see someone using a capital xi in an equation, just observe them quietly to learn as much as you can before they return to their home planet.

comics.title: Rounding
comics.altText: I've developed a novel propulsion system powered by loss of precision in unit conversion.

comics.title: Headline Words
comics.altText: Roundly-condemned headlinese initiative shuttered indefinitely.

comics.title: Chorded Keyboard
comics.altText: And even though it all went wrong / I'll stand before the lord of song / with nothing on my tongue but 'I don't understand, I swear I backed up my keyboard config before messing with it'

comics.title: Data Trap
comics.altText: It's important to make sure your analysis destroys as much information as it produces.

comics.title: Health Stats
comics.altText: You will live on forever in our hearts, pushing a little extra blood toward our left hands now and then to give them a squeeze.

comics.title: Rest and Fluids
comics.altText: Remember not to take it easy. Put a hot washcloth on your forehead, remain standing, and breathe dry air while taking lots of histamines. You need to give your body a chance to get sick again.

comics.title: Tractor Beam
comics.altText: Did you base the saucer shape on pop culture depictions of aliens, or was that stuff based on your ships? Does the rotational symmetry help with ... hey, where are you going?

comics.title: Sword Pull
comics.altText: Merlin really shouldn't leave his dirt bike lying around.

comics.title: Sea Chase
comics.altText: There are two rules on this ship: Never gaze back into the projection abyss, and never touch the red button labeled DYMAXION.

comics.title: What If? 2
comics.altText: CLARIFICATION: By 9/13, I mean September 13th, not the 9th day of Jancember, the cursed 13th month that exists between December and January in the transdimensional temporal plane.

comics.title: Autoresponder
comics.altText: I ADMIRE HOW YOU SET BOUNDARIES AND I HOPE YOUR COLLEAGUES RESPECT THEM! PLEASE SPARE MY LIFE!

comics.title: Alien Mission
comics.altText: Fine, we can go search the Himalayas for the Yeti ONE more time, but keep a safe altitude over the Pacific and PLEASE watch where you're going. We can't afford another Amelia Earhart incident.

comics.title: Alien Observers
comics.altText: ALERT: Human 910-25J-1Q38 has created a Youtube channel. Increase erratic jerkiness of flying by 30% until safely out of range.

comics.title: Hydraulic Analogy
comics.altText: Current (water) running through the water (wires) causes it to boil, increasing the pressure (voltage), but resisting (impeding) the flow of hydroelectricity (water currents). This is the basis for Ohm's law.

comics.title: Captain Picard Tea Order
comics.altText: We can ask the Earl for his order once he's fully extruded from the dispenser.

comics.title: Hypothesis Generation
comics.altText: Frazzled scientists are requesting that everyone please stop generating hypotheses for a little bit while they work through the backlog.

comics.title: Spinthariscope
comics.altText: Other high scorers are melt-in-your-hand aluminum-destroying gallium and tritium-powered glowsticks. Lawn darts are toward the other end.

comics.title: Language Development
comics.altText: The worst is the Terrible Twos, when they're always throwing things and shrieking, "forsooth, to bed thou shalt not take me, cur!"

comics.title: Decorative Constants
comics.altText: Arguably, the '1/2' in the drag equation is purely decorative, since drag coefficients are already unitless and could just as easily be half as big. Some derivations give more justification for the extra 1/2 than others, but one textbook just calls it 'a traditional tribute to Euler and Bernoulli.'

comics.title: Latency
comics.altText: Each SCAPDFATIAT point increases the chance that the process will involve the phrase 'by the next business day.'

comics.title: Sunshield

comics.title: Throat and Nasal Passages
comics.altText: I always felt like what the 'you are now aware of your tongue' thing needed in order to be truly enjoyable was an element of mortal peril.

comics.altText: Neither group uses iso 8601 because the big-endian enthusiasts were all at the meeting 20 years ago.

comics.title: Moonfall
comics.altText: Novel ideas and cool explosions are both good, but what I really want from a movie is novel ideas ABOUT cool explosions.

comics.title: Confounding Variables
comics.altText: You can find a perfect correlation if you just control for the residual.

comics.title: Rapid Test Results
comics.altText: A solid red area with two white lines means that you have been infected with the anti-coronavirus, COVID+19, which will cure anyone you have close contact with.

comics.title: Immunity
comics.altText: This plan may sound appealing to people who know a little about the immune system, but the drawbacks are clear to people who know a lot about the immune system and also to people who don't know anything about it.

comics.title: Turing Complete
comics.altText: Thanks to the ForcedEntry exploit, your company's entire tech stack can now be hosted out of a PDF you texted to someone.

comics.altText: Increasing-precision timestamps are the Jaws theme of incident reports.

comics.altText: Biology is really struggling; they're barely at 93% and they keep finding more ants.

comics.title: Debunking
comics.altText: Mark Zuckerberg has only neutral feelings toward Peppa Pig, who he understands is a fictional character, and he blames the coronavirus pandemic on other factors.

comics.title: Bayes' Theorem
comics.altText: P((B|A)|(A|B)) represents the probability that you'll mix up the order of the terms when using Bayesian notation.

comics.title: Heart-Stopping Texts
comics.altText: Was this your car? [looping 'image loading' animation]

comics.altText: Even if you said you were an employee of the website, if you asked for my password, I'd tell you.

comics.title: Occam
comics.altText: Oh no, Murphy just picked up the razor.

comics.title: Snack
comics.altText: Although grad students, suddenly reminded that food exists, tend to just grab and devour both without further discussion.

comics.title: Painbow Award

comics.title: Clinical Trials
comics.altText: We don't need to do a clinical trial of this change because the standard of care is to adopt new ideas without doing clinical trials.

comics.altText: "The slope will be 74° at ground level." "Okay, I think we can hack together a  ... wait, why did they specify ground level? It's 74° everywhere, right? ... Oh no, there's a whole section in the treaty labeled 'curvature.'"

comics.altText: Anna Karenina is a happy family lumper and unhappy family splitter.

comics.title: Circumappendiceal Somectomy

comics.title: USV-C

comics.title: Projecting
comics.altText: This is something we all need to work on, but especially you all.

comics.altText: It's definitely not the time to try drinking beer before liquor.

comics.title: Base Rate
comics.altText: Sure, you can talk about per-capita adjustment, but if you want to solve the problem, it's obvious that this is the group you need to focus on.

comics.altText: You'd need to keep track of so many people! Would you use, like, Excel or something? Far too fancy for a simple country nanoenzyme developer like me.

comics.altText: Gotten the Ferris wheel operator's attention

comics.altText: It's cool how, when there's a number lots of people are curious about, but which isn't easy to measure, some random guess will get cited everywhere and become the universally quoted value. Unrelatedly, did you know there are 850 trillion waves in the ocean?

comics.title: Next Slide Please
comics.altText: "I have nothing to offer but blood--next slide, please--toil--next slide, please--tears, and--next slide, please--sweat."

comics.altText: Junior astronomers hate getting put on board update duty, but someone's gotta make sure that stuff is still up there.

comics.title: In Your Classroom

comics.title: NASA Award
comics.altText: The key to discovering life on Mars is to find someone who built a camera and landed it on Mars. Then you just look through the pictures for plants and dogs and stuff.

comics.title: 90's Kid Space Program

comics.title: Vaccinated

comics.title: March 2020

comics.title: Bubble Wrap
comics.altText: I think of myself as the David Attenborough of factory mailing equipment.

comics.altText: I'm looking forward to having to worry a lot less about covid, but wouldn't mind if we worried a little more about giving each other colds. Colds are bad!

comics.altText: As if these problems weren't NP-hard enough.

comics.title: Eradication
comics.altText: When you get to hell, tell smallpox we say hello.

comics.title: Hammer Incident

comics.title: Immune Response
comics.altText: I don't care whether you win or lose, as long as you have-- ...okay, sorry, I'm being reminded I very much care whether you win or lose. I need you to win, that's very important.

comics.title: Mask Opinions
comics.altText: Although regardless of how everything else shakes out, I definitely won't mind if the norm "wear a mask if you're feeling sick" sticks around after this.

comics.title: Animal Songs

comics.title: mRNA Vaccine

comics.title: Normal Conversation

comics.title: Project Orion

comics.title: Vaccine Ordering

comics.title: Tower of Babel

comics.title: Appliances
comics.altText: If you had an oven bag and a dryer that runs unusually hot, I guess you could in theory make tumbled eggs.

comics.title: Hug Count
comics.altText: I've never been that big of a hug person, but it turns out I'm not quite this small of a hug person either.

comics.title: 1/100,000th Scale World
comics.altText: The floor should be slightly curved, but we haven't figured out artificial gravity yet, so for now we just added a trace intoxicating gas to the air that messes with your inner ear and gives you a sense that the ground is tilting away from you.

comics.title: 1/10,000th Scale World

comics.title: Viral Vector Immunity
comics.altText: We've secretly replaced this customer's instant coffee with our patented substitute. Let's see what she ... uh oh, I think she spotted us through the window. Now she's getting something from the closet ... oh jeez, she has a sword! Run!!

comics.title: First Thing

comics.title: 2020 Election Map
comics.altText: There are more Trump voters in California than Texas, more Biden voters in Texas than New York, more Trump voters in New York than Ohio, more Biden voters in Ohio than Massachusetts, more Trump voters in Massachusetts than Mississippi, and more Biden voters in Mississippi than Vermont.

comics.altText: *refresh* Aww, still in Kalamazoo. *refresh* Aww, still in Kalamazoo.

comics.title: I Just Don't Trust Them
comics.altText: I believe in getting immunity the old-fashioned way: By letting a bat virus take control of my lungs and turn my face into a disgusting plague fountain while my immune system desperately Googles 'how to make spike protein antibodies'.

comics.title: Wonder Woman 1984
comics.altText: 'Wait, why would you think a movie set in 1984 would do drive-ins as a retro promotion?' 'You know, 80s stuff. Drive-in movies. Britney Spears doing the hustle. Elvis going on Ed Sullivan and showing off his pog collection.' 'What year were you born, again?'

comics.title: Covid Precaution Level
comics.altText: It's frustrating to calibrate your precautions when there's only one kind of really definitive feedback you can get, you can only get it once, and when you do it's too late.

comics.title: Contiguous 41 States
comics.altText: Linguists, settling some inscrutable grudge, have been steadily sneaking more backdated synonyms for 'sharing borders' into the dictionary. They've added 'contiguous,' 'coterminous,' 'conterminous,' and next year they're adding 'conterguous.'

comics.title: Presidential Middle Names
comics.altText: The bottom of the list remains unchanged. Poor Rutherford Birchard Hayes.

comics.title: Cyber Cafe
comics.altText: Since we haven't really settled on a name for those online hangout/work spaces that try to recreate the experience of cafes, and I love confusion, I'm going to start calling them 'cyber cafes' or 'internet cafes.'

comics.title: Life Before the Pandemic

comics.title: Unread

comics.altText: After changing it back and forth several times and consulting with internet linguist Gretchen McCulloch, I settled on "ok" in my book How To, but I'm still on the fence. Maybe I should just switch to "oK."

comics.altText: "Wait, how often are you getting bitten by snakes? And why are you boiling water?" "Dunno, the CDC people keep showing up with complicated questions about the 'history of the property' and 'possible curses' but I kinda tune them out. At least one of them offered me the flu shot."

comics.altText: I laugh at the software as if I'm 100% confident that it's 2019.

comics.title: Chemistry Nobel
comics.altText: Most chemists thought the lanthanides and actinides could be inserted in the sixth and seventh rows, but no, they're just floating down at the bottom with lots more undiscovered elements all around them.

comics.altText: I want to sell apples but I'm still working on getting the machine to do the cutting and grafting.

comics.title: Drone Fishing
comics.altText: Today's consumers who order their drones off the internet don't know the joy of going out in nature and returning with a drone that you caught yourself, whose angry owners you fought off with your own two hands.

comics.title: Mavis Beacon
comics.altText: There are actually lowercase-like 'oldstyle' forms of normal numbers with more pronounced ascenders and descenders, which is why some numbers like '5' in books sometimes dangle below the line. But the true capital numbers remain the domain of number maven Mavis Beacon.

comics.altText: It's not my fault I haven't had a chance to measure the curvature of this particular universe.

comics.altText: Lots of people called their ships unsinkable before the Titanic. Voicing your hubris doesn't make failure more likely, just more memorable.

comics.title: Earth-Like Exoplanet
comics.altText: Fire is actually a potential biosignature, since it means something is filling the atmosphere with an unstable gas like oxygen. If we find a planet covered in flames, it might be an indicator that it supports life. Or used to, anyway, before the fire.

comics.altText: Trust me, you don't want to get on the wrong side of the paramilitary enforcement arm of the International Earth Rotation and Reference Systems Service.

comics.title: Nice To E-Meet You
comics.altText: I'm inside your head and I hate it. Please let me out.

comics.altText: I also have an utterance. Less of an utterance and more of an incantation. Less of an incantation and more of a malediction. Less of a malediction and more of a Word of Power. Less of a Word of Power and more of an Unforgivable Curse.

comics.title: Serena Versus the Drones
comics.altText: After the test, she said that if she had a choice, she wouldn’t defend herself against drones using a tennis ball and racket, though she would absolutely pick them over other sports equipment. But, she added, "Drones don't bother me."

comics.title: Old Game Worlds
comics.altText: Ok, how many coins for a cinnamon roll?

comics.title: E Scooters
comics.altText: Obviously battery technology and prices have driven a lot of the scooter explosion, but I feel like Dean Kamen must be at least a little grumpy about how much people laughed at the idea of the Segway.

comics.title: Geologic Time
comics.altText: Ok, well, we'll be sure to pay you sometime soon, geologically speaking.

comics.title: Dark Matter
comics.altText: To detect dark matter, we just need to build a bird feeder that spins two squirrels around the rim in opposite directions at relativistic speeds and collides them together.

comics.title: Cumulonimbus
comics.altText: The rarest of all clouds is the altocumulenticulostratonimbulocirruslenticulomammanoctilucent cloud, caused by an interaction between warm moist air, cool dry air, cold slippery air, cursed air, and a cloud of nanobots.

comics.title: Unpopular Opinions
comics.altText: I wasn't a big fan of 3 or Salvation, so I'm trying to resist getting my hopes up too much for Dark Fate, but it's hard. I'm just a sucker for humans and robots traveling through time to try to drive trucks into each other, apparently.

comics.title: Icon Swap
comics.altText: Someone's probably working on an eBook app where, if you stop reading right before some plot twist happens, the app will wait a while and then send you a breaking news alert about what's happening, prompting you to open it and read the next few pages to learn more.

comics.title: When I'm Back at a Keyboard
comics.altText: [after typing 1,500 words on feathered dinosaurs, paleontology, sexism, lava, and dinosaurs as animals rather than movie monsters] Sorry to cut it short, I'm on my phone. When I'm back at a keyboard, I can give you another 5,000 words.

comics.title: Inbox
comics.altText: Rome's declaration of war against Carthage was sent from a no-reply address, so Hannibal had to cross the Alps to deliver his "UNSUBSCRIBE" response in person.

comics.title: Spreadsheets
comics.altText: My brother once asked me if there was a function to produce a calendar grid from a list of dates in Google Sheets. I replied with a single-cell formula that took in a list of dates and outputted a calendar. It used SEQUENCE(), REGEXMATCH(), and a double-nested ARRAYFORMULA(), and it locked up the browser for 15 seconds every time it ran. I think he learned a lot about asking me things.

comics.title: NWS Warnings
comics.altText: Kind of rude of them to simultaneously issue an EVACUATION - IMMEDIATE alert, a SHELTER IN PLACE alert, and a 911 TELEPHONE OUTAGE alert.

comics.title: Expiration Date High Score
comics.altText: "Wait, we've MOVED since 2010. How on Earth did--" "Look, some of us were just born to be champions."

comics.altText: "Mostly it means that I'm acutely aware that the kid one table over coughed as the server walked past with our food."

comics.title: How Hacking Works
comics.altText: If only somebody had warned them that the world would roll them like this.

comics.title: Motivated Reasoning Olympics
comics.altText: [later] I can't believe how bad corruption has become, especially given that our league split off from the statewide one a month ago SPECIFICALLY to protest this kind of flagrantly biased judging.

comics.title: Stack
comics.altText: Gotta feel kind of bad for nation-state hackers who spend years implanting and cultivating some hardware exploit, only to discover the entire target database is already exposed to anyone with a web browser.

comics.title: Chernobyl
comics.altText: You know when you can't hear your speakers, and you keep turning various volume controls up higher and higher in confusion, and then someone hits the mute button and there's a deafening blast of sound? That's basically what happened at Chernobyl.

comics.altText: If I really focus, I can distinguish between John Steinbeck and John Updike, or between Gore Vidal and Vidal Sassoon, but not both at once.

comics.title: An Apple a Day
comics.altText: Even the powerful, tart Granny Smith cultivar is proving ineffective against new Gran-negative doctors.

comics.title: Ken Burns Theory
comics.altText: Some of the KBCU ancillary works try a little too hard to tie everything together. Doris Kearns Goodwin, the sports journalist featured in "Baseball," was somehow ALSO a famous historian who wrote bestselling biographies of Lincoln AND Johnson? Unrealistic.

comics.title: Comments

comics.title: Qualifiers
comics.altText: [20 minutes later] ", hi."

comics.title: Diploma Legal Notes
comics.altText: If you're planning to challenge the royal family, you should probably wait 6-8 weeks, since a number of the younger ones have diplomas and Kate was actually on the varsity lightsaber team at St Andrews.

comics.title: Ufo
comics.altText: "It's a little low for a weather balloon; it might be some other kind." "Yeah. Besides, I know I'm the alien conspiracy guy, but come on--the idea that the government would care about hiding something so mundane as atmospheric temperature measurement is too ridiculous even for me."

comics.title: Swimming
comics.altText: "You don't know how high above you the sky goes, but you're not freaking out about that." "Well, NOW I am!"

comics.title: Motivation
comics.altText: What's even worse is, a month ago they transferred me to work on the game I was already playing, and suddenly I found myself procrastinating by playing the one I'd been assigned before. It's possible they're onto me and this is all part of the plan.

comics.title: Effects of High Altitude
comics.altText: If she'd lived in Flagstaff (elevation 6,903 feet), Cruella de Vil would only have needed 89 dalmatians for her coat.

comics.title: Westerns
comics.altText: Sitting here idly trying to figure out how the population of the Old West in the late 1800s compares to the number of Red Dead Redemption 2 players.

comics.title: A/B

comics.title: XKeyboarCD

comics.title: Alternate Histories
comics.altText: "So their universe wouldn't have the iconic photo of a screaming Truman being hoisted aloft by the newspaper-printing machinery..."

comics.title: Cubesat Launch
comics.altText: Luckily, the damages were partly offset by the prize money we got from accidentally winning the nearby water skiing championship tournament.

comics.title: Appendicitis

comics.title: Waiting for the But
comics.altText: Listen, I'm not a fan of the Spanish Inquisition OR predatory multi-level marketing schemes...

comics.altText: When I was looking at the box, I should have thought more about what "360 degrees of freedom" meant.

comics.altText: Eventually, every epidemiologist becomes another statistic, a dedication to record-keeping which their colleagues sincerely appreciate.

comics.title: UI vs UX
comics.altText: U[unprintable glyph]: The elements a higher power uses to bend that moral arc. U[even more unprintable glyph]: The higher power's overall experience bending that moral arc.

comics.title: Reinvent the Wheel
comics.altText: Right now it's a bicycle wheel, so we've had to move to lighter vehicles, but the reduced overhead is worth it. There was one week when a wheel of cheese got dangerously close to the first page, though.

comics.title: Email Settings
comics.altText: What are all these less-than signs? What's an HREF? Look, we know you live in a fancy futuristic tech world, but not all of us have upgraded to the latest from Sun Microsystems.

comics.title: Wanna See the Code?
comics.altText: And because if you just leave it there, it's going to start contaminating things downstream even if no one touches it directly.

comics.title: Text Entry
comics.altText: I like to think that somewhere out there, there's someone whose personal quest is lobbying TV providers to add an option to switch their on-screen keyboards to Dvorak.

comics.title: Election Commentary
comics.altText: This really validates Jones's strategy of getting several thousand more votes than Smith. In retrospect, that was a smart move; those votes were crucial.

comics.title: M87 Black Hole Size Comparison
comics.altText: I think Voyager 1 would be just past the event horizon, but slightly less than halfway to the bright ring.

comics.title: Too Much Talking
comics.altText: Next time I go, I'm going to prepare a whole bunch of opinions that I'm sure are good, and make everyone sit quietly while I run through them.

comics.title: EHT Black Hole Picture
comics.altText: [five years later] Ok, it seems we were accidentally zoomed in slightly too far. But imagine there's a cool-looking twisted accretion disc just outside this black square!

comics.title: Percentage Styles
comics.altText: In a tribute to classical Latin, I started pronouncing it 'per-kent.' Eventually my friends had to resort to spritzing me with a water bottle like a cat to train me out of it.

comics.altText: As far as "being in the pocket of Big Egg" goes, I think the real threat is Chansey.
