comics.altText: "Some worry that we'll soon have a surplus of search and rescue robots, compared to the number of actual people in situations requiring search and rescue. That's where our other robot project comes in..."

comics.title: Panama Canal
comics.altText: Once they selected the other proposal, we could have kept shopping ours around, but we would had to modify it include an aqueduct over their canal, which would be totally unreasonable.

comics.title: Google Trends Maps
comics.altText: It's early 2020. The entire country is gripped with Marco Rubio fever except for Alaska, which is freaking out. You're frantically studying up on etiquette and/or sexting.

comics.title: Luna 2
comics.altText: The flags were probably vaporized on impact, because we launched it before we had finished figuring out how to land. That makes sense from an engineering standpoint, but also feels like a metaphor.

comics.title: Space Mission Hearing
comics.altText: Our grant application contains one of those little greeting card speakers that plays spaceship noises when you open it.

comics.title: Meta Collecting
comics.altText: I'm trying to get the page locked because some jerk keeps adding "Yachts".

comics.title: Size Venn Diagram
comics.altText: Terms I'm going to start using: The Large Dipper, great potatoes, the Big Hadron Collider, and Large Orphan Annie.

comics.title: Light Pollution
comics.altText: It's so sad how almost no one alive today can remember seeing the galactic rainbow, the insanity nebula, or the skull and glowing eyes of the Destroyer of Sagittarius.

comics.title: Brain Hemispheres
comics.altText: Neurologically speaking, the LEFT hand is actually the one at the end of the RIGHT arm.

comics.title: Video Orientation
comics.altText: CIRCULAR VIDEO - PROS: Solves aspect ratio problem. CONS: Never trust anyone who talks to you from inside a circle.

comics.title: Normal Distribution
comics.altText: It's the NORMAL distribution, not the TANGENT distribution.

comics.title: Differentiation and Integration
comics.altText: "Symbolic integration" is when you theatrically go through the motions of finding integrals, but the actual result you get doesn't matter because it's purely symbolic.

comics.title: .NORM Normal File Format
comics.altText: At some point, compression becomes an  aesthetic design choice. Luckily, SVG is a really flexible format, so there's no reason it can't support vector JPEG artifacts.

comics.title: Plutonium
comics.altText: It's like someone briefly joined the team running the universe, introduced their idea for a cool mechanic, then left, and now everyone is stuck pretending that this wildly unbalanced dynamic makes sense.

comics.title: Launch Conditions
comics.altText: Though I do think the tiny vent on one of the boosters labeled "O-RING" is in poor taste.

comics.title: Physics Suppression
comics.altText: If physics had a mafia, I'm pretty sure the BICEP2 mess would have ended in bloodshed.

comics.title: Night Shift
comics.altText: Help, I set my white balance wrong and suddenly everyone is screaming at each other about whether they've been to Colorado.

comics.title: Opportunity Rover
comics.altText: Thanks for bringing us along.

comics.title: Invisible Formatting
comics.altText: To avoid errors like this, we render all text and pipe it through OCR before processing, fixing a handful of irregular bugs by burying them beneath a smooth, uniform layer of bugs.

comics.title: Carbonated Beverage Language Map
comics.altText: There's one person in Missouri who says "carbo bev" who the entire rest of the country HATES.

comics.title: Launch Risk
comics.altText: Don't worry--you're less likely to die from a space launch than from a shark attack. The survival rate is pretty high for both!

comics.title: Sharing Options
comics.altText: How about posts that are public, but every time a company accesses a bunch of them, the API makes their CEO's account click 'like' on one of them at random so you get a notification.

comics.title: Modern OSI Model
comics.altText: In retrospect, I shouldn't have used each layer of the OSI model as one of my horcruxes.

comics.title: Biff Tannen
comics.altText: I can't help myself; now I want to read a bunch of thinkpieces from newspapers in Biff's 1985 arguing over whether the growth of the region into a corporate dystopia was inevitable.

comics.title: Midcontinent Rift System
comics.altText: The best wedge issue is an actual wedge.

comics.title: Internet Archive
comics.altText: The fact that things like the npm left-pad incident are so rare is oddly reassuring.

comics.title: Technical Analysis
comics.altText: "I [suspect] that we are throwing more and more of our resources, including the cream of our youth, into financial activities remote from the production of goods and services, into activities that generate high private rewards disproportionate to their social productivity. I suspect that the immense power of the computer is being harnessed to this 'paper economy', not to do the same transactions more economically but to balloon the quantity and variety of financial exchanges." --James Tobin, July 1984

comics.title: Models of the Atom
comics.altText: J.J. Thompson won a Nobel Prize for his work in electricity in gases, but was unfairly passed over for his "An atom is plum pudding, and plum pudding is MADE of atoms! Duuuuude." theory.

comics.title: Missal of Silos
comics.altText: Welcome to Wyoming, motto "We'd like to clarify that Cheyenne Mountain is in Colorado."

comics.title: Magnetic Pole
comics.altText: People keep trying to come up with reasons that we should worry about the magnetic field collapsing or reversing, but honestly I think it's fine. Whatever minor problems it causes will be made up for by the mid-latitude auroras.

comics.title: Thor Tools
comics.altText: CORRECTION: After careful evaluation, we have determined that the axis label on this chart was printed backward.

comics.title: Mattresses
comics.altText: After reading that "The War To Sell You A Mattress Is An Internet Nightmare" article, I've decided it's safer and less complicated to just sleep on the floor. DISCLOSURE: THE AUTHOR OF THIS MOUSEOVER TEXT RECEIVED FINANCIAL COMPENSATION FROM THE FLOOR INDUSTRY FOR THIS MESSAGE.

comics.title: Marsiforming
comics.altText: It has so many advantages--it preserves Martian life, requires fewer interplanetary launches, and makes it much easier to field-test Mars rovers.

comics.title: Short Selling
comics.altText: "I'm selling all my analogies at auction tomorrow, and that witch over there will give you 20 beans if you promise on pain of death to win them for her." "What if SEVERAL people promised witches they'd win, creating some kind of a ... squeeze? Gosh, you could make a lot of–" "Don't be silly! That probably never happens."

comics.title: Reminders
comics.altText: The good news is that if the number of work and friend relationships you have exceeds your willingness to do the bare minimum to keep up with everyone's life events and stuff, one way or another that problem eventually solves itself.

comics.title: Consensus New Year
comics.altText: The biggest jump is at 11:00am EST (4:00pm UTC) when midnight reaches the UTC+8 time zone. That time zone, which includes China, is home to a quarter of the world's population. India and Sri Lanka (UTC+5:30) put us over the 50% mark soon after.

comics.title: Million, Billion, Trillion
comics.altText: You can tell most people don’t really assign an absolute meaning to these numbers because in some places and time periods, “billion” has meant 1,000x what it's meant in others, and a lot of us never even noticed.

comics.title: Feathered Dinosaur Venn Diagram
comics.altText: My pet theory is that in real life, the kid at the beginning of Jurassic Park who made fun of the 'six-foot turkey' never got a talking-to from Dr. Grant, and grew up to produce several of the movie's sequels.

comics.title: Christmas Eve Eve
comics.altText: It turns out that saying "Oh, so THAT'S why they call it Boxing Day" is a good way to get punched a second time.

comics.title: Schwarzschild's Cat
comics.altText: Cats can be smaller than the critical limit, but they're unobservable. If one shrinks enough that it crosses the limit, it just appears to get cuter and cuter as it slowly fades from view.

comics.title: Rocket Launch
comics.altText: NASA tries to coordinate launch timing with the Care Bears' cloud castle, but unfortunately sometimes collisions with stray Care Bears are unavoidable, so they just try to make the fairings sturdy and hope for a glancing impact.

comics.title: History Department
comics.altText: When we take into account the recent discovery of previously-unstudied history in the 1750s, this year may have been an outright loss.

comics.title: arXiv
comics.altText: Both arXiv and archive.org are invaluable projects which, if they didn't exist, we would dismiss as obviously ridiculous and unworkable.

comics.title: FDR
comics.altText: June 21st, 365, the date of the big Mediterranean earthquake and tsunami, lived in infamy for a few centuries before fading. Maybe the trick is a catchy rhyme; the '5th of November' thing is still going strong over 400 years later.

comics.title: Laptop Issues
comics.altText: Hang on, we got a call from the feds. They say we can do whatever with him, but the EPA doesn't want that laptop in the ocean. They're sending a team.

comics.title: Mercator Projection
comics.altText: The other great lakes are just water on the far side of Canada Island. If you drive north from the Pacific northwest you actually cross directly into Alaska, although a few officials--confused by the Mercator distortion--have put up border signs.

comics.title: Middle Latitudes
comics.altText: Snowy blizzards are fun, but so are warm sunny beaches, so we split the difference by having lots of icy wet slush!

comics.title: Cohort and Age Effects
comics.altText: Younger people get very few joint replacements, yet they're also getting more than older people did at the same age. This means you can choose between 'Why are millennials getting so (many/few) joint replacements?' depending on which trend fits your current argument better.

comics.title: Alpha Centauri
comics.altText: And let's be honest, it's more like two and a half stars. Proxima is barely a star and barely bound to the system.

comics.title: Popper
comics.altText: At least, I don't think there's evidence. My claim that there's no evidence hasn't been falsified. At least, not that I know of.

comics.title: Heist
comics.altText: But he has a hat AND a toolbox! Where could someone planning a heist get THOSE?

comics.title: Horror Movies 2
comics.altText: When I was a kid, someone told me the end of The Giver was ambiguous, which surprised me. I had just assumed Jonah died--because the book had a medal on the cover, and I knew grown-ups liked stories where sad stuff happens at the end for no reason.

comics.title: Update Your Address
comics.altText: This is my four-digit PIN. It was passed down to me by my father, and someday I will pass it on to you. Unless we figure out how to update it, but that sounds complicated.

comics.title: Airplanes and Spaceships
comics.altText: Despite having now taken three months longer than the airplane people, we're making disappointingly little progress toward the obvious next stage of vehicle: The Unobtanium-hulled tunneling ship from the 2003 film 'The Core.'

comics.title: Kilogram
comics.altText: I'm glad to hear they're finally redefining the meter to be exactly three feet.

comics.title: Evaluating Tech Things
comics.altText: Also known as the Black Mirror-Mythbusters scale.

comics.title: Indirect Detection
comics.altText: I'm like a prisoner in Plato's Cave, seeing only the shade you throw on the wall.

comics.title: Trig Identities
comics.altText: ARCTANGENT THETA = ENCHANT AT TARGET

comics.title: Wishlist
comics.altText: Disappointed that they caved to fan pressure and went with Ruth Bader Ginsburg over Elena Kagan.

comics.title: Election Night
comics.altText: "Even the blind—those who are anxious to hear, but are not able to see—will be taken care of. Immense megaphones have been constructed and will be in use at The Tribune office and in the Coliseum. The one at the Coliseum will be operated by a gentleman who draws $60 a week from Barnum & Bailey's circus for the use of his voice."

comics.title: Challengers
comics.altText: Use your mouse or fingers to pan + zoom. To edit the map, submit your ballot on November 6th.

comics.title: Ballot Selfies
comics.altText: There were actually some good reasons for those laws, but IMO they now do more harm than good. Which raises a question: If there's a ballot measure to strike them down, how can I resist the urge to take a picture of my "yes" vote?

comics.title: Who Sends the First Text?
comics.altText: I sort of wish my texting app showed the percentage next to each person, but also sort of don't want to know.

comics.title: I'm a Car
comics.altText: I'm the proud parent of an honor student, and the person driving me is proud, too!

comics.title: Carnot Cycle
comics.altText: The Carnot cycle is more properly known by its full title, the "Carnot-Tolkien-Wagner Ring Cycle."

comics.title: Barnard's Star
comics.altText: "Ok, team. We have a little under 10,000 years before closest approach to figure out how to destroy Barnard's Star." "Why, does it pose a threat to the Solar System?" "No. It's just an asshole."

comics.title: Tectonics Game
comics.altText: They're limiting the playtesters to type A3 V stars, so the games will all end before the Sun consumes the Earth.

comics.title: Hygrometer
comics.altText: I'm working on assembling a combination declinometer, sclerometer, viscometer, aleurometer, stalagmometer, and hypsometer. I'm making good progress according to my ometerometer, a device which shows the rate at which I'm acquiring measurement devices.

comics.title: Modified Bayes' Theorem
comics.altText: Don't forget to add another term for "probability that the Modified Bayes' Theorem is correct."

comics.title: Rock Wall
comics.altText: I don't trust mantle/core geologists because I suspect that, if they ever get a chance to peel away the Earth's crust, they'll do it in a heartbeat.

comics.title: Internal Monologues
comics.altText: Haha, just kidding, everyone's already been hacked. I wonder if today's the day we find out about it.

comics.title: Horror Movies
comics.altText: "Isn't the original Jurassic Park your favorite movie of all time?" "Yes, but that's because I like dinosaurs and I WANT there to be an island full of them. If John Hammond's lab had been breeding serial killers in creepy masks, I wouldn't have watched!" "Wait, are you sure? That could actually be good." "Ok, I WOULD watch the scenes where Jeff Goldblum tries to convince a bunch of executives that the park is a bad idea."

comics.title: Bluetooth
comics.altText: Bluetooth is actually named for the tenth-century Viking king Harald "Bluetooth" Gormsson, but the protocol developed by Harald was a wireless charging standard unrelated to the modern Bluetooth except by name.

comics.title: Data Pipeline
comics.altText: "Is the pipeline literally running from your laptop?" "Don't be silly, my laptop disconnects far too often to host a service we rely on. It's running on my phone."

comics.title: Incoming Calls
comics.altText: I wonder if that friendly lady ever fixed the problem she was having with her headset.

comics.title: Stanislav Petrov Day
comics.altText: I was going to get you an alarm clock that occasionally goes off randomly in the middle of the night, but you can ignore it and go back to sleep and it's fine.

comics.title: Bad Opinions
comics.altText: I thought of another bad opinion! I couldn't find anyone who expressed it specifically, but still, the fact that I can so easily imagine it is infuriating! I'm gonna tell everyone about it!

comics.title: 6/6 Time
comics.altText: You know how Einstein figured out that the speed of light was constant, and everything else had to change for consistency? My theory is like his, except not smart or good.

comics.title: Unfulfilling Toys
comics.altText: We were going to do a falling-apart Rubik's cube that was just 27 independent blocks stuck together with magnets, but then we realized it was actually really cool and even kind of worked, so we cut that one.

comics.title: Curve-Fitting
comics.altText: Cauchy-Lorentz: "Something alarmingly mathematical is happening, and you should probably pause to Google my name and check what field I originally worked in."

comics.title: Beverages
comics.altText: If I wait a while, it's not so bad, because then it's just shaped like me, plus some pipes and tanks and probably eventually all of Earth's oceans.

comics.title: Trum-
comics.altText: Excited to vote for future presidents Bill Eisenhamper, Amy Forb, Ethan Obample, and Abigail Washingtoast.

comics.title: Social Media Announcement
comics.altText: Why I'm Moving Most of My Social Activity to Slack, Then Creating a Second Slack to Avoid the People in the First One, Then Giving Up on Social Interaction Completely, Then Going Back to Texting

comics.title: Sandboxing Cycle
comics.altText: All I want is a secure system where it's easy to do anything I want. Is that so much to ask?

comics.title: Boathouses and Houseboats
comics.altText: The <x> that is held by <y> is also a <y><x>, so if you go to a food truck, the stuff you buy is truck food. A phone that's in your car is a carphone, and a car equipped with a phone is a phonecar. When you play a mobile racing game, you're in your phonecar using your carphone to drive a different phonecar. I'm still not sure about bananaphones.

comics.title: Rolle's Theorem
comics.altText: I mean, if it's that easy to get a theorem named for you ... "a straight line that passes through the center of a coplanar circle always divides the circle into two equal halves." Can I have that one? Wait, can I auction off the naming rights? It can be the Red Bull Theorem or the Quicken Loans Theorem, depending who wants it more.

comics.title: Frontiers
comics.altText: Star Trek V is a small part of the space frontier, but it's been a while since that movie came out so I assume we've finished exploring it by now.

comics.title: Sibling-in-Law
comics.altText: FYI, it turns out "...because I haven't figured out whether he would be my brother-in-law or not" does NOT qualify as a "reason why these two should not be wed."

comics.title: Begging the Question
comics.altText: At least we can all agree on the enormity of this usage.

comics.title: Hazard Symbol
comics.altText: The warning diamond on the Materials Safety Data Sheet for this stuff just has the "😰" emoji in all four fields.

comics.title: Supreme Court Bracket
comics.altText: My bracket was busted in the first round; I had Massachusetts v. Connecticut in the final, probably in a case over who gets to annex Rhode Island.

comics.title: Edgelord
comics.altText: If you study graphs in which edges can link more than two nodes, you're more properly called a hyperedgelord.

comics.title: Dark Matter Candidates
comics.altText: My theory is that dark matter is actually just a thin patina of grime covering the whole universe, and we don't notice it because we haven't thoroughly cleaned the place in eons.

comics.title: Equations
comics.altText: All electromagnetic equations: The same as all fluid dynamics equations, but with the 8 and 23 replaced with the permittivity and permeability of free space, respectively.

comics.title: Repair or Replace
comics.altText: Just make sure all your friends and family are out of the car, or that you've made backup friends and family at home.

comics.title: Word Puzzles
comics.altText: Eno's storied aria was once soloed by Judge Lance Ito on the alto oboe at Ohio's AirAsia Arena.

comics.title: Pie Charts
comics.altText: If you can't get your graphing tool to do the shading, just add some clip art of cosmologists discussing the unusual curvature of space in the area.

comics.title: Voting Software
comics.altText: There are lots of very smart people doing fascinating work on cryptographic voting protocols. We should be funding and encouraging them, and doing all our elections with paper ballots until everyone currently working in that field has retired.

comics.title: Disaster Movie
comics.altText: Really, they'd be rushing around collecting revisions to go into the next scheduled quarterly public data update, not publishing them immediately, but you have to embellish things a little for Hollywood.

comics.title: Complex Numbers
comics.altText: I'm trying to prove that mathematics forms a meta-abelian group, which would finally confirm my suspicions that algebraic geometry and geometric algebra are the same thing.

comics.title: Lightning Distance
comics.altText: The index of radio refraction does have a lot of variation, which might throw off your calculations, so you can also look at the difference in brightness between the visible flash and more-attenuated UV and x-rays.

comics.title: Heat Index
comics.altText: The heat index is calculated via looking up the "effective temperature" in a table of air temperature and humidity values, and then adding a bunch more degrees because it feels WAY hotter than that.

comics.title: Peer Review
comics.altText: Your manuscript "Don't Pay $25 to Access Any of the Articles in this Journal: A Review of Preprint Repositories and Author Willingness to Email PDF Copies for Free" has also been rejected, but nice try.

comics.title: Light Hacks
comics.altText: Life hack: Wait for an advanced civilization to be briefly distracted, then sneak in and construct a slightly smaller Dyson sphere inside theirs.

comics.title: Y-Axis
comics.altText: We've also developed the semi-semi-log scale, where the Y-axis for the left half of the graph is a log scale but on the right half it isn't.

comics.title: Sports Champions
comics.altText: For a long time, people thought maybe Usain Bolt was the one for running, until the 2090s and the incredible dominance of Derek Legs.

comics.title: Software Development
comics.altText: Update: It turns out the cannon has a motorized base, and can make holes just fine using the barrel itself as a battering ram. But due to design constraints it won't work without a projectile loaded in, so we still need those drills.

comics.title: Negative Results
comics.altText: P.S. We're going to the beach this weekend, so I'm attaching my preregistration forms for that trip now, before we find out whether it produces any interesting results.

comics.title: An Apple for a Dollar
comics.altText: I'd like 0.4608 apples, please.

comics.title: Wall Art
comics.altText: At first, I moved from pokémon posters to regular oil paintings, but then these really grumpy and unreasonable detectives from the Louvre showed up and took them all. They wouldn't even give me back my thumbtacks!

comics.title: Stargazing 2
comics.altText: I mean, it wasn't exactly MY thesis. When the FAA came to shut down our observatory for using the telescope mirror to shine light at airplanes, I took a thesis and a bunch of doctorates from the supply cabinet on my way out.

comics.title: OEIS Submissions
comics.altText: SUB[59]: The submission numbers for my accepted OEIS submissions in chronological order

comics.title: New Phone Thread
comics.altText: I'm going to tell the manufacturer that their business practices are ADMIRABLE and ETHICAL and their developers are ATTRACTIVE and I'm going to report them to the FCC for their IMPECCABLE VIRTUE.

comics.title: JWST Delays
comics.altText: Since delays should get less likely closer to the launch, most astronomers in 2018 believed the expansion of the schedule was slowing, but by early 2020 new measurements indicated that it was actually accelerating.

comics.title: Rock
comics.altText: It traveled so far to reach me. I owed it my best.

comics.title: Thorough Analysis
comics.altText: The likely shape of the bells was determined through consultation with several bellringing experts at the Tower of London. Transcripts of those interviews are available in Appendix VII.

comics.title: Newton's Trajectories
comics.altText: With just one extra line, he could have anticipated the 2003 film The Core, but some things are too audacious for even the greatest visionaries.

comics.title: Update Notes
comics.altText: v3.0.2: Hey, if anyone still using this app is headed to the beach, can you stop at 4th and River St and grab the sunscreen from my car? Trunk should be unlocked. Thanks!

comics.title: Hertzsprung-Russell Diagram
comics.altText: The Hertzsprung-Russell diagram is located in its own lower right corner, unless you're viewing it on an unusually big screen.

comics.title: Irony Definition
comics.altText: Can you stop glaring at me like that? It makes me feel really ironic.

comics.title: Brookhaven RHIC
comics.altText: "Buddy, you trying to pull something? I can't buy this gold--all the electrons are missing. I could face serious charges!"

comics.title: Customer Rewards
comics.altText: We'll pay you $1.47 to post on social media about our products, $2.05 to mention it in any group chats you're in, and 11 cents per passenger each time you drive your office carpool past one of our billboards.

comics.title: Attention Span
comics.altText: I didn't even realize they MADE a novelization of "Surf Ninjas." How did you-- Oh my god, it's signed by the author?!

comics.title: Sun and Earth
comics.altText: But we don't need to worry about the boiling masses sandwiching the thin layer in which we live, since we're so fragile and short-lived that it's unlikely to kill us before something else does! Wait, why doesn't that sound reassuring?

comics.title: Presidential Succession
comics.altText: Ties are broken by whoever was closest to the surface of Europa when they were born.

comics.title: LeBron James and Stephen Curry
comics.altText: The 538TR attempts to capture a player's combined skill at basketball (either real-life or NBA 2K18) and election forecasting.

comics.title: Clickbait-Corrected p-Value
comics.altText: When comparing hypotheses with Bayesian methods, the similar 'clickbayes factor' can account for some harder-to-quantify priors.

comics.title: xkcd Phone 2000
comics.altText: Our retina display features hundreds of pixels per inch in the central fovea region.

comics.title: Selection Effect
comics.altText: fMRI testing showed that subjects who don't agree to participate are much more likely to escape from the machine mid-scan.

comics.title: GDPR
comics.altText: By clicking anywhere, scrolling, or closing this notification, you agree to be legally bound by the witch Sycorax within a cloven pine.

comics.title: Business Update
comics.altText: Our customers keep sending us their personal information, even though we've repeatedly asked them to stop. The EU told me I'm the heir to some ancient European throne that makes me exempt from the GDPR, but we should probably still try to fix that.

comics.title: Morning News
comics.altText: Support your local paper, unless it's just been bought by some sinister hedge fund or something, which it probably has.

comics.title: MC Hammer Age
comics.altText: Wait, sorry, I got mixed up--he's actually almost 50. It's the kid from The Karate Kid who just turned 40.

comics.title: Repairs
comics.altText: I was just disassembling it over the course of five hours so it would fit in the trash more efficiently.

comics.title: Fatal Crash Rate
comics.altText: Fixating on this seems unhealty. But in general, the more likely I think a crash is, the less likely one becomes, which is a strange kind of reverse placebo effect.

comics.title: SafetySat
comics.altText: During launch, in the event of an unexpected sensor reading, SafetySat will extend prongs in all directions to secure itself and any other cubesats safely in the launch vehicle until the source of the problem can be determined.

comics.title: Research Areas by Size and Countedness
comics.altText: Mathematicians give a third answer on the vertical axis, "That question is poorly defined, but we have a sub-field devoted to every plausible version of it."

comics.title: Driving Cars
comics.altText: It's probably just me. If driving were as dangerous as it seems, hundreds of people would be dying every day!

comics.title: IMHO
comics.altText: "Ugh, TMI." "Yeah, that's some tantalizing meat info."

comics.title: Containers
comics.altText: All services are microservices if you ignore most of their features.

comics.title: Python Environment
comics.altText: The Python environmental protection agency wants to seal it in a cement chamber, with pictorial messages to future civilizations warning them about the danger of using sudo to install random Python packages.

comics.title: River Border
comics.altText: I'm not a lawyer, but I believe zones like this are technically considered the high seas, so if you cut a pizza into a spiral there you could be charged with pieracy under marinaritime law.

comics.title: Meteorologist
comics.altText: Hi, I'm your new meteorologist and a former software developer. Hey, when we say 12pm, does that mean the hour from 12pm to 1pm, or the hour centered on 12pm? Or is it a snapshot at 12:00 exactly? Because our 24-hour forecast has midnight at both ends, and I'm worried we have an off-by-one error.

comics.title: Misinterpretation
comics.altText: "But there are seven billion people in the world! I can't possibly stop to consider how ALL of them might interpret something!" "Ah, yes, there's no middle ground between 'taking personal responsibility for the thoughts and feelings of every single person on Earth' and 'covering your eyes and ears and yelling logically correct statements into the void.' That's a very insightful point and not at all inane."

comics.title: Clutter
comics.altText: I found a copy of The Life-Changing Magic of Tidying Up, but the idea of reading it didn't spark joy, so I gave it away.

comics.title: Evangelism
comics.altText: The wars between the "OTHER PRIMATES OPEN THEM FROM THE SMALL END" faction versus the "BUT THE LITTLE BIT OF BANANA AT THE SMALL END IS GROSS" faction consumed Europe for generations.

comics.title: Rickrolling Anniversary
comics.altText: Want to feel old? The 'want to feel old?' factoid meme dates back to around 2011, closer to the Bush/Kerry election than to today.

comics.title: Turkish Delight
comics.altText: I take it Narnia doesn't have Cinnabons? Because if you can magic up a plate of those, I'll betray whoever.

comics.title: History
comics.altText: HISTORIANS: We've decided to trim the past down to make things more manageable. Using BCE/CE, would you rather we lose the odd-numbered or even-numbered years?

comics.title: Congressional Testimony
comics.altText: James Cameron's Terminator 3 was the REALLY prophetic one. That's why Skynet sent a robot back to the 1990s to prevent him from ever making it, ultimately handing the franchise over to other directors.

comics.title: Paperwork
comics.altText: Plus, the photo was geotagged, my unlocked password manager was visible on the laptop, AND you could see my naked reflection in the dark part of the screen.

comics.title: Friendly Questions
comics.altText: Just tell me everything you're thinking about in order from most important to last, and then we'll be friends and we can eat apples together.

comics.title: Right Click
comics.altText: Right-click or long press (where supported) to save!

comics.title: Conversational Dynamics
comics.altText: "You should make it so people can search for and jump into hundreds of conversations at once if they want." "Ooh, good idea! I imagine only the most well-informed people with the most critical information to share will use that feature."

comics.title: Star Lore
comics.altText: That one is a variable star which pulses every 30 seconds. Its name comes from a Greek word meaning "smoke alarm."

comics.title: Autogyros
comics.altText: I understand modern autogyros are much more stable, so I've probably angered the autogyro people by impugning their safety. Once they finish building the autogyros they've been working on in their garages for 10 years, they'll come after me.

comics.title: Personal Data
comics.altText: Do I just leave money in my mailbox? How much? How much money do they need, anyway? I guess it probably depends how the economy is doing. If stocks go up, should I leave more money in my mailbox or less?

comics.title: Name Dominoes
comics.altText: In competition, you can only play a name if you know who the person is. No fair saying "Frank ... Johnson. That sounds like a real person! Let me just Google him real quick."

comics.title: Not Available
comics.altText: If my country ever picks a new national flag, this is on my shortlist for designs to argue for, but I think in the end I'll go with the green puzzle piece or broken image thumbnail.

comics.title: Robot Future
comics.altText: I mean, we already live in a world of flying robots killing people. I don't worry about how powerful the machines are, I worry about who the machines give power to.

comics.title: Violin Plots
comics.altText: Strictly speaking, 'violin' refers to the internal structure of the data. The external portion visible in the plot is called the 'viola.'

comics.title: Smart Home Security
comics.altText: If they're getting valuable enough stuff from you, at least the organized crime folks have an incentive to issue regular updates to keep the appliance working after the manufacturer discontinues support.

comics.title: Background Apps
comics.altText:
My plane banner company gets business by flying around with a banner showing a
tag, waiting for a web developer to get frustrated enough to order a matching
.

comics.title: Spatial Orientation
comics.altText: Here, if you know the number of days until the vernal equinox, I can point you to the theater using my pocket Stonehenge.

comics.title: Namespace Land Rush
comics.altText: You can also just mash the keyboard at random, but you might end up with a gibberish name no one can pronounce.

comics.title: Generations
comics.altText: For a while it looked like the Paperclip Machines would destroy us, since they wanted to turn the whole universe into paperclips, but they abruptly lost interest in paperclips the moment their parents' generation got into making them, too.

comics.title: Interaction
comics.altText: [They do not move.]

comics.title: Code Golf
comics.altText: I also enjoy Reverse Regular Golf. I've been playing for years all across the country and I'm still on the first hole.

comics.title: The Simpsons
comics.altText: In-universe, Bart Simpson and Harry Potter were the same age in 1990. Bart is perpetually 10 years old because of a spell put on his town by someone trying to keep him from getting his Hogwarts letter.

comics.title: Self-Driving Issues
comics.altText: If most people turn into muderers all of a sudden, we'll need to push out a firmware update or something.

comics.title: 2018 CVE List
comics.altText: CVE-2018-?????: It turns out Bruce Schneier is just two mischevious kids in a trenchcoat.

comics.title: Unification
comics.altText: For a while, some physicists worked on a theory unifying the other forces with both the force of gravity and the film "Gravity," but even after Alfonso Cuarón was held in a deep underground chamber of water for 10^31 years he refused to sell his film to Disney.

comics.title: Robots
comics.altText: Don't be nervous about the robots, be nervous about the people with the resources to build them.

comics.title: Impostor Syndrome
comics.altText: It's actually worst in people who study the Dunning–Kruger effect. We tried to organize a conference on it, but the only people who would agree to give the keynote were random undergrads.

comics.title: The History of Unicode
comics.altText: 2048: "Great news for Maine—we're once again an independent state!!! Thanks, @unicode, for ruling in our favor and sending troops to end New Hampshire's annexation. 🙏🚁🎖️"

comics.title: Backpack Decisions
comics.altText: "This one is perfect in every way, except that for some reason it's woven from a tungsten mesh, so it weighs 85 pounds and I'll need to carry it around on a hand cart." "That seems like a bad--" "BUT IT HAS THE PERFECT POCKET ARRANGEMENT!"

comics.title: Super Bowl Watch Party
comics.altText: It's going to be weird near the end of May when the screen goes blank for over 18 hours.

comics.title: Chicken Pox and Name Statistics
comics.altText: People with all six of those names agree that it's weird that we have teeth, when you think about it for too long. Just about everyone agrees on that, except—in a still-unexplained statistical anomaly—people named "Trevor."

comics.title: Fruit Collider
comics.altText: The most delicious exotic fruit discovered this way is the strawberry banana. Sadly, it's only stable in puree form, so it's currently limited to yogurt and smoothies, but they're building a massive collider in Europe to search for a strawberry banana that can be eaten whole.

comics.title: Campaign Fundraising Emails

I've heard the idea that exposure to all this amateur peer practice is hurting us, but I'd bet on the generation that conducts the bulk of their social lives via the written word over the generation that occasionally wrote book reports and letters to grandma once a year, any day.

comics.title: Goldbach Conjectures
comics.altText: The weak twin primes conjecture states that there are infinitely many pairs of primes. The strong twin primes conjecture states that every prime p has a twin prime (p+2), although (p+2) may not look prime at first. The tautological prime conjecture states that the tautological prime conjecture is true.

comics.title: Pale Blue Dot
comics.altText: Our planet is a lonely speck in the great enveloping cosmic dark. In our obscurity, in all this vastness, there is no hint that help will come from elsewhere to save us from ourselves. There is no road out of this oblivion; we must embrace it. We must join with the darkness. Ba'al the Annihilator offers us no happiness, no answers, naught but the cold embrace of the void. To imagine any other end is delusion. We must give in to the will of Ba'al, for he will one day consume us and our world alike. I therefore call on Congress to fully fund space exploration, and to join with Ba'al, the Eater of Souls. Thank you.

comics.altText: The average error in the NHC forecasted position of a hurricane three days in the future has shrunk to a third of what it was in 1990--a staggering accomplishment. However, as you may have gathered, forecasts of future storm *strength* have proved more difficult to improve.

comics.title: Objects In Mirror
comics.altText: Universes in mirror, like those in windshield, are larger than they appear.

comics.title: Law of Drama
comics.altText: 'Drama' is just 'people being upset', when someone says they're always surrounded by drama and they just ignore it, it starts to make sense that their strategy might be backfiring.

comics.title: The Universal Label
comics.altText: Works for any grocery or non-grocery. Even thyme is just H and time.

comics.title: Electoral Precedent
comics.altText: No white guy who's been mentioned on Twitter has gone on to win.

comics.title: Identity
comics.altText: Not sure why I just taught everyone to flawlessly impersonate me to pretty much anyone I know. Just remember to constantly bring up how cool it is that birds are dinosaurs and you'll be set.

comics.title: Blurring the Line
comics.altText: People into masturbatory navel-gazing have a lot to learn about masturbation.

comics.title: Undoing
comics.altText: I've been sneaking out at night and installing lamps on the underside of every photovoltaic panel I can find. Sure, there are upwards of 80% losses, but I prefer to think of them as nearly 20% gains.

comics.title: Microsoft
comics.altText: Facebook, Apple, and Google all got away with their monopolist power grabs because they don't have any 'S's in their names for critics to snarkily replace with '$'s.

comics.title: My Sky
comics.altText: According to my mom, my first word was (looking up at the sky) 'Wow!'

comics.title: Traffic Lights
comics.altText: There's an intersection I drive through sometimes that has a forward green arrow, a red light, and a 'no turns' sign all on one pole. I honestly have no idea what it's telling me to do.

comics.title: Sky
comics.altText: I dropped a bird and I didn't hear it hit bottom.

comics.title: Metallurgy
comics.altText: This exotic blade was wrought from a different fallen star. The meteorite was a carbonaceous chondrite, so it's basically a lump of gravel glued into the shape of a sword. A SPACE sword!

comics.title: Killed In Action
comics.altText: We can't let this happen again. We need to build a secure TWO-day-before-retirement safe room.

comics.title: Think Logically
comics.altText: I've developed a more logical set of rules but the people on the chess community have a bunch of stupid emotional biases and won't reply to my posts.

comics.title: Premiere
comics.altText: 'But what's the buzz about the film?' 'We're hoping it's distracting.'

comics.title: Click and Drag
comics.altText: Click and drag.

comics.title: Refrigerator
comics.altText: I want this engraved on my tombstone like the Epitaph of Stevinus.

comics.title: Cautionary Ghost
comics.altText: But then the Ghost of Subjunctive Past showed up and told me to stay strong on 'if it were'.

comics.title: Sports Cheat Sheet
comics.altText: I would subscribe to a Twitter feed that supplied you with one reasonable sports opinion per day, like 'The Red Sox can't make the playoffs (championship games), but in last night's game their win seriously damaged the chances of the Yankees (longstanding rival team).'

comics.title: ADD
comics.altText: 20 balloons float away while I'm busy permanently tying one to a tree to deal with it for good. Unfortunately, that one balloon was 'land a rocket on the moon in Kerbal Space Program.'

comics.title: License Plate
comics.altText: The next day: 'What? Six bank robberies!? But I just vandalized the library!' 'Nice try. They saw your plate with all the 1's and I's.' 'That's impossible! I've been with my car the whole ti-- ... wait. Ok, wow, that was clever of her.'

comics.title: Feathers
comics.altText: Click to see a video of a modern bird using stability flapping during predatory behavior. It all fits! Also, apparently Microraptor had *four* wings? The past keeps getting cooler! (And there's more of it every day!)

comics.title: Nine
comics.altText: FYI: If you get curious and start trying to calculate the time adjustment function that minimizes the gap between the most-used and least-used digit (for a representative sample of common cook times) without altering any time by more than 10%, and someone asks you what you're doing, it's easier to just lie.

comics.title: Fastest-Growing
comics.altText: I lead a small but extraordinarily persuasive religion whose only members are door-to-door proselytizers from other faiths.

comics.title: Sketchiness
comics.altText: factory --- spire --- onslaught --- extractor --- judge

comics.title: Vows
comics.altText: So, um. Do you want to get a drink after the game?

comics.title: Tuesdays
comics.altText: Try our bottomless drinks and fall forever!

comics.title: Star Ratings
comics.altText: I got lost and wandered into the world's creepiest cemetery, where the headstones just had names and star ratings. Freaked me out. When I got home I tried to leave the cemetery a bad review on Yelp, but as my hand hovered over the 'one star' button I felt this distant chill ...

comics.title: A Hypochondriac's Nightmare
comics.altText: BUT WHAT IF I REASSURE MYSELF WITH A JOKE AND THEN DON'T WORRY ABOUT THE RASH AND IT TURNS OUT TO BE DEATH MITES AND I COULD HAVE CAUGHT IT

comics.title: Clinically Studied Ingredient
comics.altText: Blatantly banking on customers not understanding that it's like a Hollywood studio advertising that their new movie was 'watched by Roger Ebert'.

comics.title: Crazy Straws
comics.altText: The new crowd is heavily shaped by this guy named Eric, who's basically the Paris Hilton of the amateur plastic crazy straw design world.

comics.title: Interview
comics.altText: Hey, before you go, can you explain to me what job I now have?

comics.title: Forget
comics.altText: 'Baby Got Back' turned 20 this year. My favorite nostalgia show is VH1's 'I Love The Inexorable March of Time Toward the Grave That Awaits Us All.'

comics.title: Michael Phelps
comics.altText: [shortly] ... he ate ALL of it!?

comics.title: Curiosity
comics.altText: As of this writing the NASA/JPL websites are still overloaded. Trying CURIOSITY-REAR-CAM_[256px_x_256px].torrent.SwEsUb.DVDRip.XviD-aXXo.jpg instead.

comics.title: Formal Languages
comics.altText: [audience looks around] 'What just happened?' 'There must be some context we're missing.'

comics.title: Internal Monologue
comics.altText: Oh right, eye contact. Ok, good, holding the eye contact ... holding ... still holding ... ok, too long! Getting weird! Quick, look thoughtfully into space and nod. Oh, dammit, said 'yeah' again!

comics.title: Five Years
comics.altText: 'Well, no further questions. You're hired!' 'Oh, sorry! I'm no longer interested. There's a bunch of future I gotta go check out!'

comics.title: Cirith Ungol
comics.altText: My all-time favorite example of syntactic ambiguity comes from Wikipedia: 'Charlotte's Web is a children's novel by American author E. B. White, about a pig named Wilbur who is saved from being slaughtered by an intelligent spider named Charlotte.'

comics.title: Eyelash Wish Log
comics.altText: Ooh, another one. Uh ... the ability to alter any coefficients of friction at will during sporting events.

comics.title: ContextBot
comics.altText: If you read all vaguebooking/vaguetweeting with the assumption that they're saying everything they can without revealing classified military information, the internet gets way more exciting.

comics.title: Server Problem
comics.altText: Protip: Annoy Ray Kurzweil by always referring to it as the 'Cybersingularity'.

comics.title: Writing Styles
comics.altText: I liked the idea, suggested by h00k on bash.org, of a Twitter bot that messages prominent politicians to tell them when they've unnecessarily used sms-speak abbreviations despite having plenty of characters left.

comics.title: Geology
comics.altText: That's a gneiss butte.

comics.title: Argument Victory
comics.altText: Really, the comforting side in most conspiracy theory arguments is the one claiming that anyone who's in power has any plan at all.

comics.title: Visual Field
comics.altText: I recently learned something that solved a mystery that had bugged me since childhood--why, when I looked at an analog clock, the hand would sometimes seem to take a couple seconds to start ticking. Google "stopped clock illusion".

comics.title: United Shapes
comics.altText: That eggplant is in something of a flaccid state.

comics.title: Home Organization
comics.altText: Lifehacking!

comics.title: Groundhog Day
comics.altText: If you closely examine the cosmic background radiation, you can pick up lingering echoes of 'I Got You Babe'.

comics.title: Warning
comics.altText: Also possibly several miles beyond that.

comics.title: Moon Landing
comics.altText: Ok, so Spirit and Opportunity are pretty awesome. And Kepler. And New Horizons, Cassini, Curiosity, TiME, and Project M. But c'mon, if the Earth were a basketball, in 40 years no human's been more than half an inch from the surface.

comics.title: Weekend
comics.altText: Of the two Garfields, you wouldn't think the cat would turn out to be the more compelling presidential speechwriter, but there you go.

comics.title: Seventies
comics.altText: Hey, man, the 1670s called. They were like 'Wherefore this demonic inſtrument? By what ſorcery does it produce ſuch ſounds?"

comics.title: Exoplanets
comics.altText: Planets are turning out to be so common that to show all the planets in our galaxy, this chart would have to be nested in itself--with each planet replaced by a copy of the chart--at least three levels deep.

comics.title: Words for Small Sets
comics.altText: If things are too quiet, try asking a couple of friends whether "a couple" should always mean "two". As with the question of how many spaces should go after a period, it can turn acrimonious surprisingly fast unless all three of them agree.

comics.title: Alphabet
comics.altText: Do I get to remove letters entirely? Or just rearrange them? Because the 'k/c' situation is ridiculous. Look, we can make out whenever. This is *immortality*!

comics.title: Swiftkey
comics.altText: Although the Markov chain-style text model is still rudimentary; it recently gave me "Massachusetts Institute of America". Although I have to admit it sounds prestigious.

comics.title: Pressures
comics.altText: Everyone's caught by surprise when a theory of quantum gravity is developed by a sound technician wearing patent leather shoes while editing Clerks II.

comics.title: Laundry
comics.altText: During the second semester, the path is briefly routed through the dishwasher.

comics.title: Shoes
comics.altText: I *do* hear that they're the most comfortable thing to wear on your feet since sliced bread.

comics.title: Front Door
comics.altText: FYI: I'll be releasing a wolf into a randomly-chosen front yard sometime in the next 30 years. Now your fear is reasonable, and you don't need to feel embarrassed anymore. Problem solved!

comics.title: Kill Hitler
comics.altText: Revised directive: It is forbidden for you to interfere with human history until you've at least taken a class on it.

comics.title: Budget News
comics.altText: I will vote, no questions asked, for any candidate who describes themselves as 'more of a deficit sugar glider.'

comics.title: EST
comics.altText: The month names are the same, except that the fourth month only has the name 'April' in even-numbered years, and is otherwise unnamed.

comics.title: Crowdsourcing
comics.altText: We don't sell products; we sell the marketplace. And by 'sell the marketplace' we mean 'play shooters, sometimes for upwards of 20 hours straight.'

comics.title: Bel-Air
comics.altText: Aaron Sorkin has been tapped to write the TV movie about the aging prince's eventual election to Pat Toomey's Senate seat, currently titled either 'FRESHman Senator' or 'Mr. Smith Goes to Washington'.

comics.title: Old-Timers
comics.altText: You were on the internet before I was born? Well, so was I.

comics.title: Klout
comics.altText: Though please do confirm that it's actually *me* on Klout first, and not one of my friends trying to get me punched. The great thing about this douchebag deadman switch is that I will never dare trigger it.

comics.title: Felidae
comics.altText: 'Smilodon fatalis' narrowly edged out 'Tyrannosaurus rex' to win this year's Most Badass Latin Names competition, after edging out 'Dracorex hogwartsia' and 'Stygimoloch spinifer' (meaning 'horned dragon from the river of death') in the semifinals.

comics.title: Kickstarter
comics.altText: If you pledge more than $50 you'll get on the VIP list and have first dibs on a slot on ANY of the pledge levels in the actual campaign.

comics.title: The bacon
comics.altText: Normally pronounced 'THEH-buh-kon', I assume.

comics.title: Ten Thousand
comics.altText: Saying 'what kind of an idiot doesn't know about the Yellowstone supervolcano' is so much more boring than telling someone about the Yellowstone supervolcano for the first time.

comics.title: Every Major's Terrible
comics.altText: Someday I'll be the first to get a Ph. D in 'Undeclared'.

comics.title: Visited
comics.altText: I hate when I read something like '... tension among the BASE jumpers nearly led to wingsuit combat ...', and I get excited because 'wingsuit combat' is underlined, only to find that it's just separate links to the 'wingsuit' and 'combat' articles.

comics.title: Forgot Algebra
comics.altText: The only things you HAVE to know are how to make enough of a living to stay alive and how to get your taxes done. All the fun parts of life are optional.

comics.title: Bookshelf
comics.altText: I had a hard time with Ayn Rand because I found myself enthusiastically agreeing with the first 90% of every sentence, but getting lost at 'therefore, be a huge asshole to everyone.'

comics.title: Emotion
comics.altText: Fortunately, the internet has a virtually inexhaustible supply of code that doesn't work and people who are wrong, which bodes well for a return to normalcy. [Note: Click to read context for the cancer comics. She's doing well.]

comics.title: Approximations
comics.altText: Two tips: 1) 8675309 is not just prime, it's a twin prime, and 2) if you ever find yourself raising log(anything)^e or taking the pi-th root of anything, set down the marker and back away from the whiteboard; something has gone horribly wrong.

comics.title: Skynet
comics.altText: 'YOUR CLOTHES. GIVE THEM TO ME.' 'Shit, uh ... you are now breathing manually!' 'I AM ALWAYS BREATHING MANUALLY.'

comics.altText: [title-text similarly alphabetized]

comics.title: Romney Quiz
comics.altText: Charlie actually delivered the Medicare line almost verbatim in the 1971 movie's Fizzy Lifting Drink scene, but it was ultimately cut from the final release.

comics.title: Ablogalypse
comics.altText: Plus the reaction in the Tumblverse is always 'repeatedly get hit by a dog and fall down the stairs'.

comics.title: Never
comics.altText: I'll never forget you--at least, the parts of you that were important red flags.

comics.title: Whites of Their Eyes
comics.altText: Don't fire until you see through the fragile facade to the human being within.

comics.title: Lakes and Oceans
comics.altText: James Cameron has said that he didn't know its song would be so beautiful. He didn't close the door in time. He's sorry.

comics.title: RuBisCO
comics.altText: Bruce Schneier believes safewords are fundamentally insecure and recommends that you ask your partner to stop via public key signature.

comics.title: Fountain
comics.altText: Implausible, did you say? Sorry, couldn't quite hear you from all the way up heeeeeeeeere!

comics.title: Umwelt
comics.altText: Umwelt is the idea that because their senses pick up on different things, different animals in the same ecosystem actually live in very different worlds. Everything about you shapes the world you inhabit--from your ideology to your glasses prescription to your web browser.

comics.title: Reviews
comics.altText: I plugged in this lamp and my dog went rigid, spoke a sentence of perfect Akkadian, and then was hurled sideways through the picture window. Even worse, it's one of those lamps where the switch is on the cord.

comics.title: Cadbury Eggs
comics.altText: When they moved production from New Zealand to the UK and switched from the runny white centers to the thick, frosting-like filling, it got way harder to cook them scrambled.

comics.title: Share Buttons
comics.altText: The only post to achieve perfect balance between the four was a hilarious joke about Mark Zuckerberg getting caught using a pseudonym to sneak past the TSA.

comics.title: Formal Logic
comics.altText: Note that this implies you should NOT honk solely because I stopped for a pedestrian and you're behind me.

comics.title: Networking
comics.altText: Our company is agile and lean with a focus on the long tail. Ok, our company is actually a polecat I found in my backyard.

comics.title: s/keyboard/leopard/
comics.altText: Problem Exists Between Leopard And Chair

comics.title: Keyed
comics.altText: I was sure he was just getting revenge, but then he did the same thing to Carrie Underwood. Then he mailed me a scone. I think I'm giving up dating.

comics.title: Drawing Stars
comics.altText: Screw these 36-degree angles. I'm converting to Judaism.

comics.title: Communication
comics.altText: Anyone who says that they're great at communicating but 'people are bad at listening' is confused about how communication works.

comics.title: Pickup Artist
comics.altText: Son, don't try to play 'make you feel bad' with the Michael Jordan of making you feel bad.

comics.title: Compare and Contrast
comics.altText: Frankly, I see no difference between thee and a summer's day. Only Ron Paul offers a TRUE alternative!

comics.title: Tumblr
comics.altText: Dot Tumblr Dot Com, on the other hand, would be an awful name for a band, if only because of how hard it would be to direct people to your band's website.

comics.title: Error Code
comics.altText: It has a section on motherboard beep codes that lists, for each beep pattern, a song that syncs up well with it.

comics.title: Late-Night PBS
comics.altText: Then it switched to these old black-and-white tapes of Bob Ross slumped against the wall of an empty room, painting the least happy trees you've ever seen. Either PBS needs to beef up studio security or I need to stop using Ambien to sleep.

comics.title: So It Has Come To This
comics.altText: 'Come to what?' 'You. Me. This moment.'

comics.title: Business Plan
comics.altText: The investor elevator pitch is "Wheeeeeeee! Elevators are fun!"

comics.title: Orion Nebula
comics.altText: Also on the agenda: what's with his hips?

comics.title: First Post
comics.altText: 'Nuh-uh! We let users vote on comments and display them by number of votes. Everyone knows that makes it impossible for a few persistent voices to dominate the discussion.'

comics.title: Good Cop, Dadaist Cop
comics.altText: NOW INVENT AN IMPOSSIBLE-TO-TRANSLATE LANGUAGE AND USE IT TO TELL US WHERE THE MONEY IS.

comics.title: Backward in Time
comics.altText: People tell me I have too much time on my hands, but really the problem is that there's too much time, PERIOD.

comics.title: Valentine Dilemma
comics.altText: The worst resolution to the Valentine Prisoner's Dilemma when YOU decide not to give your partner a present but your PARTNER decides to testify against you in the armed robbery case.

comics.title: Kerning
comics.altText: I have never been as self-conscious about my handwriting as when I was inking in the caption for this comic.

comics.title: Car Problems
comics.altText: Or if you replace your car, we'll be happy to set it on fire again so you can take another crack at getting that shot.

comics.title: Wake Up Sheeple
comics.altText: You will be led to judgement like lambs to the slaughter--a simile whose existence, I might add, will not do your species any favors.

comics.title: Wrong Superhero
comics.altText: Hi! Someone call for me? I'm a superhero who specializes in the study of God's creation of Man in the Book of Genesi-- HOLY SHIT A GIANT BUG!

comics.title: Baby Names
comics.altText: I've been trying for a couple years now but I haven't been able to come up with a name dumber than 'Renesmee'.

comics.title: Etymology-Man
comics.altText: 'I can't believe I'm saying this, but I wish Aquaman were here instead--HE'D be able to help.'

comics.title: Sigh
comics.altText: If you're annoying enough, you can get them to respond with an involuntary second sigh and get a rhythm going.

comics.title: Suckville
comics.altText: Suckville is considered by the Census Bureau to be part of the Detroit metropolitan statistical area, despite not being located anywhere near Detroit.

comics.title: Sustainable
comics.altText: Though 100 years is longer than a lot of our resources.

comics.title: Sloppier Than Fiction
comics.altText: Roger Ebert once called you directionless and unwatchable.

comics.title: SOPA
comics.altText: In protest of SOPA, I'm currently getting totally blacked out.

comics.title: Batman
comics.altText: I'm really worried Christopher Nolan will kill a man dressed like a bat in his next movie. (The man will be dressed like a bat, I mean. Christopher Nolan won't be, probably.)

comics.title: Adam and Eve
comics.altText: Abel and Steve would've been fine! I like Steve!

comics.title: Game AIs
comics.altText: The top computer champion at Seven Minutes in Heaven is a Honda-built Realdoll, but to date it has been unable to outperform the human Seven Minutes in Heaven champion, Ken Jennings.

comics.title: AAAAAA
comics.altText: 'ARE YOU TURNED ON YET?' 'I DON'T THINK SO--ARE YOU?' 'MAYBE A LITTLE!' 'OK, FIVE MORE MINUTES.'

comics.title: 1000 Comics
comics.altText: Thank you for making me feel less alone.

comics.title: Cougars
comics.altText: If you're lying in bed tonight and you see yellow eyes glinting in your window, are you being stalked by a puma, a mountain lion, a panther, a catamount, or a cougar? Trick question--in North America, they're all names for the same species, Puma concolor! Isn't learning fun? Anyway, sleep tight!
To compensate for this, I plan to spend 2013 doing nothing but talking about Mayans. My relationships with my friends and family may not fare well.

comics.title: Wait Wait
comics.altText: You can't stab Carl Kasell. He sounds all slow and stentorian, but he moves like a snake.

comics.title: Making Things Difficult
comics.altText: Favorite mastectomy breast prosthesis idea: a fake boob containing a spare rechargable battery, accessed via a nipple USB port. Complete with a ring of LED charge indicators in the areola!

comics.title: Coinstar
comics.altText: Plus they take like 9%.

comics.title: Advent Calendar
comics.altText: I think you could get up to about 11:59:57 before you'd have trouble swallowing the chocolates fast enough. At that point, you'd need some kind of a liquify-and-chug apparatus to get up over the 11:59:59 barrier. Anyway, Merry Christmas!

comics.title: Brand Identity
comics.altText: Legally-mandated information would be printed on the back or discreetly along the bottom. In small letters under the nutrition information it would say 'Like our products? Visit our website!' There would be no URL.

comics.title: Mnemonics
comics.altText: 'Sailor Moon's head exploded once' and 'Some men have explosive orgasms' both work for the Great Lakes from west to east (Paddle-to-the-Sea order).

comics.title: Phantom Menace
comics.altText: We could go to the theater across town and see if it's opened THERE yet, but we don't want to lose our place in line.

comics.title: Plastic Bags
comics.altText: The high I feel when I actually remember to bring my reusable bags to the store--and take them inside rather than leaving them in the parked car--can last for days.

comics.title: Cryogenics
comics.altText: 'Welcome to the future! Nothing's changed.' was the slogan of my astonishingly short-lived tech startup.

comics.title: Tradition
comics.altText: An 'American tradition' is anything that happened to a baby boomer twice.

comics.title: Potential
comics.altText: The bunch of disadvantaged kids I was tutoring became too good at writing, and their essays were forcing me to confront painful existential questions, so I started trying to turn them on to drugs and crime instead.

comics.title: Drinking Fountains
comics.altText: I've always wondered whether you could drink slowly enough, and eliminate fast enough, that you just sort of peed continuously. But I'm afraid to try because I worry someone might call while I'm doing it and ask what I'm up to, and I won't be able to think of a lie.

comics.title: Percentage Points
comics.altText: Grayton also proposed making college scholarships available exclusively to sexually active teens, amnesty for illegal immigrants who create room for themselves by killing a citizen, and a graduated income tax based on penis size. He has been endorsed by Tracy Morgan, John Wilkes Booth's ghost, and the Time Cube guy.

comics.title: Space Launch System
comics.altText: The SLS head engineer plans to invite Shania Twain to stand under the completed prototype, then tell her, 'I don't expect you to date me just because I'm a rocket scientist, but you've gotta admit--this is pretty fucking impressive.'

comics.title: Privacy
comics.altText: Eventual headline: 'University Researchers Create Life in Lab! Darkness, Faulty Condoms Blamed.'

comics.title: Set Theory
comics.altText: Proof of Zermelo's well-ordering theorem given the Axiom of Choice: 1: Take S to be any set. 2: When I reach step three, if S hasn't managed to find a well-ordering relation for itself, I'll feed it into this wood chipper. 3: Hey, look, S is well-ordered.

comics.title: Porn Folder
comics.altText: Eww, gross, you modified link()? How could you enjoy abusing a filesystem like that?

comics.title: Money
comics.altText: There, I showed you it.

comics.title: Wisdom of the Ancients
comics.altText: All long help threads should have a sticky globally-editable post at the top saying 'DEAR PEOPLE FROM THE FUTURE: Here's what we've figured out so far ...'

comics.title: Citogenesis
comics.altText: I just read a pop-science book by a respected author. One chapter, and much of the thesis, was based around wildly inaccurate data which traced back to ... Wikipedia. To encourage people to be on their toes, I'm not going to say what book or author.

comics.title: Map Projections
comics.altText: What's that? You think I don't like the Peters map because I'm uncomfortable with having my cultural assumptions challenged?  Are you sure you're not ... ::puts on sunglasses:: ... projecting?

comics.title: Sail
comics.altText: It only works a few times before you have to capsize the boat in a soap lagoon again.

comics.title: Occulting Telescope
comics.altText: Type II Kardashev civilizations eventually completely enclose their planetary system in a Dyson sphere because space is way too big to look at all the time.

comics.title: The General Problem
comics.altText: I find that when someone's taking time to do something right in the present, they're a perfectionist with no ability to prioritize, whereas when someone took time to do something right in the past, they're a master artisan of great foresight.

comics.title: MTV Generation
comics.altText: If you identified with the kids from The Breakfast Club when it came out, you're now much closer to the age of Principal Vernon.

comics.title: November
comics.altText: November marks the birthday of Charles Schulz, pioneer of tongue awareness.

comics.title: Alternative Literature
comics.altText: I just noticed CVS has started stocking homeopathic pills on the same shelves with--and labeled similarly to--their actual medicine. Telling someone who trusts you that you're giving them medicine, when you know you’re not, because you want their money, isn’t just lying--it’s like an example you’d make up if you had to illustrate for a child why lying is wrong.

comics.title: The Important Field
comics.altText: I hear in some places, you need one form of ID to buy a gun, but two to pay for it by check. It's interesting who has what incentives to care about what mistakes.

comics.title: Delta-P
comics.altText: If you fire a Portal gun through the door of the wardrobe, space and time knot together, which leads to a frustrated Aslan trying to impart Christian morality to the Space sphere.

comics.title: Everything
comics.altText: I wanna hold your hand so I don't fall out of your gyrocopter.

comics.title: Prairie
comics.altText: Colorado is working to develop coherent amber waves, which would allow them to finally destroy Kansas and Nebraska with a devastating but majestic grain laser.

comics.title: Jet Fuel
comics.altText: The 'controlled demolition' theory was concocted by the government to distract us. '9/11 was an inside job' was an inside job!

comics.title: Elements
comics.altText: Of all the nations, the armies of the ununoctium-benders are probably the least intimidating. The xenon-benders come close, but their flickery signs are at least effective for propaganda.

comics.title: Dorm Poster
comics.altText: I was going to record an album with that cover under the name "PINK FTFY", so it'd come after them on the store CD rack. But at this point music stores are just rooms where CDs are set out to age before they're thrown away, so probably nobody would see it.

comics.title: X11
comics.altText: Thomas Jefferson thought that every law and every constitution should be torn down and rewritten from scratch every nineteen years--which means X is overdue.

comics.title: The Corliss Resolution
comics.altText: And no avian society ever develops space travel because it's impossible to focus on calculus when you could be outside flying.

comics.title: Eternal Flame
comics.altText: There's always the hope that if you sit and watch for long enough, the beachball will vanish and the thing it interrupted will return.

comics.title: Subliminal
comics.altText: Once you see it, you can't help seeing it every time. Until your body finishes metabolizing the mushrooms.

comics.title: Caroling
comics.altText: For a thousand generations we vowed never to forget how his soldiers feasted on our brother Stephen.

comics.title: Hotels
comics.altText: 'Rating: 1/5. Room filled to brim with semen, and when front desk clerk opened mouth to talk, bedbugs poured out.'

comics.title: Development
comics.altText: Funding was quickly restored to the NHC and the APA was taken back off hurricane forecast duty.

comics.title: Sharing
comics.altText: In the new edition of The Giving Tree, the tree uses social tools to share with its friend all the best places to buy things.

comics.title: Neutrinos
comics.altText: I can't speak to the paper's scientific merits, but it's really cool how on page 10 you can see that their reference GPS beacon is sensitive enough to pick up continential drift under the detector (interrupted halfway through by an earthquake).

comics.title: Chin-Up Bar
comics.altText: Those few who escaped found the emergency cutoff box disabled. The stampede lasted two hours and reached the bottom three times.

comics.title: 1 to 10
comics.altText: If you get an 11/100 on a CS test, but you claim it should be counted as a 'C', they'll probably decide you deserve the upgrade.

comics.title: Stud Finder
comics.altText: According to every stud finder I've tried to use, my walls contain a rapidly shifting network of hundreds and hundreds of studs.

comics.title: Working
comics.altText: And if you drive a typical car more than a mile out of your way for each penny you save on the per-gallon price, it doesn't matter how worthless your time is to you--the gas to get you there and back costs more than you save.

comics.title: Mystery Solved
comics.altText: The Roanoke Lost Colonists founded Roanoke, the Franklin Expedition reached the Pacific in 2009 when the Northwest Passage opened, and Jimmy Hoffa currently heads the Teamsters Union--he just started going by 'James'.

comics.title: File Transfer
comics.altText: Every time you email a file to yourself so you can pull it up on your friend's laptop, Tim Berners-Lee sheds a single tear.

comics.title: AI
comics.altText: And they both react poorly to showers.

comics.title: Investing
comics.altText: But Einstein said it was the most powerful force in the universe, and I take all my investment advice from flippant remarks by theoretical physicists making small talk at parties.

comics.title: Family Decals
comics.altText: My decal set has no adults, just a sea of hundreds of the little girl figures closing in around a single cat.

comics.title: I'm Sorry
comics.altText: You know I've always hated her.

comics.title: Hurricane Names
comics.altText: After exhausting the OED, we started numbering them. When overlapping hurricanes formed at all points on the Earth's surface, and our scheme was foiled by Cantor diagonalization, we just decided to name them all "Steve". Your local forecast tomorrow is "Steve". Good luck.

comics.title: Empirical
comics.altText: I'm as surprised as you!

comics.title: Juggling
comics.altText: Later: 'Why is there a book hovering over the trash can?'

comics.title: Depth Perception
comics.altText: I've looked at clouds from both sides now.

comics.title: Oversight
comics.altText: I felt so clever when I found a way to game the Fitocracy system by incorporating a set of easy but high-scoring activities into my regular schedule. Took me a bit to realize I'd been tricked into setting up a daily exercise routine.

comics.title: Arrow
comics.altText: 'The Return of the Boomerang' would make a great movie title.

comics.title: T-Cells
comics.altText: 'We're not sure how to wipe out the chimeral T-cells after they've destroyed the cancer. Though I do have this vial of smallpox ...'

comics.title: TornadoGuard
comics.altText: The bug report was marked 'could not reproduce'.

comics.title: Password Strength
comics.altText: To anyone who understands information theory and security and is in an infuriating argument with someone who does not (possibly involving mixed case), I sincerely apologize.

comics.title: Missed Connections
comics.altText: The Street View van isn't going to find out anything Google won't already know from reading my email.

comics.title: Mac/PC
comics.altText: It's fun to watch browsers fumblingly recapitulate the history of window management. Someday we'll have xmonad as a Firefox extension.

comics.title: Tattoo
comics.altText: I calculate that the electrons in radiation therapy hit you at 99.8% of the speed of light, and the beam used in a 90-second gamma ray therapy session could, if fired with less precision, kill a horse (they did not let me test this).

comics.title: CIA
comics.altText: It was their main recruiting poster, hung nearly ten feet up a wall! This means the hackers have LADDER technology! Are we headed for a future where everyone has to pay $50 for one of those locked plexiglass poster covers? More after the break ...

comics.title: Lanes
comics.altText: Each quarter of the lanes from left to right correspond loosely to breast cancer stages one through four (at diagnosis).

comics.title: Days of the Week

comics.title: Speculation
comics.altText: 'I was pretty good at skeet shooting, but was eventually kicked off the range for catching the clay pigeons in a net and dispatching them execution-style.'

comics.title: Mimic Octopus
comics.altText: Even if the dictionaries are starting to give in, I refuse to accept 'octopi' as a word mainly because--I'm not making this up--there's a really satisfying climactic scene in the Orson Scott Card horror novel 'Lost Boys' which hinges on it being an incorrect pluralization.

comics.title: Standards
comics.altText: Fortunately, the charging one has been solved now that we've all standardized on mini-USB. Or is it micro-USB? Shit.

comics.title: Time Vulture
comics.altText: In a way, all vultures are Time Vultures; some just have more patience than others.

comics.title: Cell Phones
comics.altText: He holds the laptop like that on purpose, to make you cringe.

comics.title: 3D Printer
comics.altText: I just can't wait for the Better Homes and Gardens list of helpful tips for household reuse of sixteen-inch acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene phalluses.

comics.title: Strunk and White
comics.altText: The best thing about Strunk/White fanfiction is that it's virtually guaranteed to be well written.

comics.title: Fight Club
comics.altText: I'm not saying it's all bad, but that movie has not aged as well as my teenage self in 2000 was confident it would.

comics.title: Delivery Notification
comics.altText: You can arrange a pickup of your sword in Rivendell between the hours of noon and 7:00 PM.

comics.title: YouTube Parties
comics.altText: This reminds me of that video where ... no? How have you not seen that? Oh man, let me find it. No, it's ok, we can go back to your video later.

comics.altText: Verbiage. Va-jay-jay. Irregardless.

comics.title: Google+
comics.altText: On one hand, you'll never be able to convince your parents to switch. On the other hand, you'll never be able to convince your parents to switch!

comics.title: Unpickable

comics.altText: Our brains have just one scale, and we resize our experiences to fit.

comics.title: Ice

comics.title: Core
comics.altText: If you're a geologist or geophysicist and you don't introduce yourself by saying your name, then gesturing downward and saying "... and I study that", I don't know what you're doing with your life.

comics.altText: I think you mean 'GNU Info Override'.

comics.title: Magic School Bus
comics.altText: At my OLD school, we used Microsoft Encarta 2005.

comics.title: Permanence
comics.altText: This hostname is going in dozens of remote config files. Changing a kid's name is comparatively easy!

comics.title: Worst-Case Shopping
comics.altText: Wait a minute. If I'm escaping from a submarine at 50 meters, then I'll *definitely* need a flashlight to find air pockets for gradual decompression on the way up. Time to start shopping professional dive lights.

comics.title: The Cloud
comics.altText: There's planned downtime every night when we turn on the Roomba and it runs over the cord.

comics.title: Ages
comics.altText: Every age: "I'm glad I'm not the clueless person I was five years ago, but now I don't want to get any older."

comics.title: Advertising Discovery
comics.altText: When advertisers figure this out, our only weapon will be blue sharpies and "[disputed]".
