comics.title: Sports
comics.altText: Also, all financial analysis. And, more directly, D&D.

comics.title: Extended Mind
comics.altText: Wikipedia trivia: if you take any article, click on the first link in the article text not in parentheses or italics, and then repeat, you will eventually end up at "Philosophy".

comics.title: Darmok and Jalad
comics.altText: I wonder how often Patrick Stewart has Darmok flashbacks when talking to Star Trek fans.

comics.title: Temperature
comics.altText: And the baby has a fever.

comics.title: Religions
comics.altText: But to us there is but one God, plus or minus one. --1 Corinthians 8:6±2.

comics.title: Number Line
comics.altText: The Wikipedia page "List of Numbers" opens with "This list is incomplete; you can help by expanding it."

comics.title: Chain of Command
comics.altText: Themistocles said his infant son ruled all Greece -- "Athens rules all Greece; I control Athens; my wife controls me; and my infant son controls her." Thus, nowadays the world is controlled by whoever buys advertising time on Dora the Explorer.

comics.title: Elevator Inspection
comics.altText: Even governmental elevator inspectors get bored halfway through asking where the building office is.

comics.title: Marie Curie
comics.altText: Although not permanently.

comics.title: Teaching Physics
comics.altText: Space-time is like some simple and familiar system which is both intuitively understandable and precisely analogous, and if I were Richard Feynman I'd be able to come up with it.

comics.title: Progeny
comics.altText: I tell my children 'it's not whether you win or lose, it's how you play the game.' I'm trying to take the edge off their competitive drive to ensure that I can always beat them.

comics.title: 65 Years
comics.altText: The universe is probably littered with the one-planet graves of cultures which made the sensible economic decision that there's no good reason to go into space--each discovered, studied, and remembered by the ones who made the irrational decision.

comics.title: Null Hypothesis
comics.altText: Hell, my eighth grade science class managed to conclusively reject it just based on a classroom experiment. It's pretty sad to hear about million-dollar research teams who can't even manage that.

comics.title: Movie Ages
comics.altText: If you're 15 or younger, then just remember that it's nevertheless probably too late to be a child prodigy.

comics.title: Etymology
comics.altText: For some reason, my childhood suspension of disbelief had no problem with the fact that this ancient galaxy is full of humans, but was derailed by language. There's no Asia OR Europe there, so where'd they get all the Indo-European roots?

comics.title: Turtles
comics.altText: You're a turtle!

comics.title: Heaven
comics.altText: If you've never had sex, this is what it feels like. Complete with the brief feeling of satisfaction, followed by ennui, followed by getting bored and trying to make it happen again.

comics.title: Future Timeline
comics.altText: Not shown: the approximately 30,000 identical, vaguely hysterical articles titled "WHITE PEOPLE IN [THE US/BRITAIN] TO BECOME MINORITY BY [YEAR]!", which came up for basically any year I put in.

comics.altText: $1600 / 1386153BR 3BATH, MODERN SLIDING DOORS, GUEST ROOMS, GARBAGE DISPOSAL. FREE MANDATORY PARKING (ENFORCED). CONVENIENT TO ALDERAAN.

comics.altText: And given how much of my stuff they go through, they definitely know where I live.

comics.altText: 'We can also use middle names in place of first pet's names, but yours is something incomprehensible about dropping tables.'

comics.title: Pain Rating

comics.title: Probability
comics.altText: My normal approach is useless here, too.

comics.title: Headache
comics.altText: I'm only willing to visit placid lakes, salt flats, and painting exhibits until the world's 3D technology improves.

comics.title: Lamp
comics.altText: 'That was definitely not in my top three wishes.' 'Who said anything about YOUR wishes?'

comics.title: Model Rail
comics.altText: I don't know what's more telling--the number of pages in the Wikipedia talk page argument over whether the 1/87.0857143 scale is called "HO" or "H0", or the fact that within minutes of first hearing of it I had developed an extremely strong opinion on the issue.

comics.title: Beauty
comics.altText: The best hugs are probably from hagfish, which can extrude microscopic filaments that convert a huge volume of water around them to slime in seconds. Instant cozy blanket!

comics.title: Trapped
comics.altText: Socrates could've saved himself a lot of trouble if he'd just brought a flashlight, tranquilizer gun, and a bunch of rescue harnesses.

comics.title: 2009 Called
comics.altText: 2017 called, but I couldn't understand what they were saying over all the screams.

comics.title: Time Management
comics.altText: I never trust anyone who's more excited about success than about doing the thing they want to be successful at.

comics.title: FPS Mod
comics.altText: Wait, that second one is a woman?  ... wait, if that bothers me, then why doesn't ... man, this game is no fun anymore.

comics.title: Fairy Tales
comics.altText: Goldilocks' discovery of Newton's method for approximation required surprisingly few changes.

comics.title: Charity
comics.altText: I usually respond to someone else doing something good by figuring out a reason that they're not really as good as they seem. But I've been realizing lately that there's an easier way to handle these situations, and it involves zero internet arguments.

comics.title: Advertising
comics.altText: I remember the exact moment in my childhood when I realized, while reading a flyer, that nobody would ever spend money solely to tell me they wanted to give me something for nothing. It's a much more vivid memory than the (related) parental Santa talk.

comics.title: Server Attention Span
comics.altText: They have to keep the adjacent rack units empty. Otherwise, half the entries in their /var/log/syslog are just 'SERVER BELOW TRYING TO START CONVERSATION *AGAIN*.' and 'WISH THEY'D STOP GIVING HIM SO MUCH COFFEE IT SPLATTERS EVERYWHERE.'

comics.title: Nolan Chart
comics.altText: Also in the right quadrant are NFPA-compliant chemical manufacturers and Sir Charles Wheatstone. Sharing the top with the internet libertarians are Nate Silver and several politically-active kite designers.

comics.title: Herpetology
comics.altText: Birds are Aves, which is part of the clade Theropoda, which is in Saurischia, which is in Dinosauria. Those birds outside our windows are dinosaurs. We can clear out the rest of our brains because we now have the best fact.

comics.title: Compass and Straightedge
comics.altText: The Greeks long suspected this, but it wasn't until April 12th of 1882 that Ferdinand von Lindemann conclusively proved it when he constructed himself the most awesome birthday party possible and nobody showed up.

comics.title: Nanobots
comics.altText: I think the IETF hit the right balance with the 128 bits thing. We can fit MAC addresses in a /64 subnet, and the nanobots will only be able to devour half the planet.

comics.title: Flying Cars
comics.altText: It's hard to fit in the backseat of my flying car with my android Realdoll when we're both wearing jetpacks.

comics.title: Major in the Universe
comics.altText: I hear Steven Levitt is writing a book analyzing A.J. Jacobs' quest to spend a year reading everything Malcolm Gladwell ever wrote. The audiobook will be narrated by Robert Krulwich of Radiolab.

comics.title: Let Go
comics.altText: After years of trying various methods, I broke this habit by pitting my impatience against my laziness. I decoupled the action and the neurological reward by setting up a simple 30-second delay I had to wait through, in which I couldn't do anything else, before any new page or chat client would load (and only allowed one to run at once). The urge to check all those sites magically vanished--and my 'productive' computer use was unaffected.

comics.title: Wisdom Teeth
comics.altText: I heard the general anesthesia drugs can cause amnesia, so when I woke up mid-extraction I started taking notes on my hand so I'd remember things later. I managed 'AWAKE BUT EVERYTHING OK' before the dental assistant managed to find and confiscate all my pens.

comics.title: Never Do This
comics.altText: I call Rule 34 on The Secret House.
Brains aside, I wonder how many poorly-written xkcd.com-parsing scripts will break on this title (or ;;"''{<<[' this mouseover text."

comics.title: Milk
comics.altText: It's not hard when you have the same thought like 40 or 50 percent of the time.

comics.title: Archimedes
comics.altText: Give a man a fish, or he will destroy the only existing vial of antidote.

comics.title: Trochee Fixation
comics.altText: If you Huffman-coded all the 'random' things everyone on the internet has said over the years, you'd wind up with, like, 30 or 40 bytes *tops*.
'Whoa, twenty-two in two hours!' 'Your site got twenty-two hundred hits in two hours?' 'No. Twenty-two. But still, that's like half the people on the internet!'

comics.title: Learning to Cook
comics.altText: And yet I never stop thinking, 'sure, these ingredients cost more than a restaurant meal, but think how many meals I'll get out of them! Especially since each one will have leftovers!'

comics.title: Consecutive Vowels
comics.altText: But the windows! What if there's a voyeur watchi-- wait, now I'm turned on too.

comics.title: Local g
comics.altText: In Rio de Janeiro in 2016, the same jump will get an athlete 0.25% higher (>1cm) than in London four years prior.

comics.title: Na
comics.altText: I hear that there are actual lyrics later on in Land of 1,000 Dances, but other than the occasional 'I said,' I've never listened long enough to hear any of them.

comics.title: World According to Americans
comics.altText: It's not our fault we caught a group on their way home from a geography bee. And they taught us that Uzbekistan is one of the world's two doubly-landlocked countries!

comics.title: Complex Conjugate
comics.altText: Fun fact: if you say this every time a professor does something to a complex-number equation that drops the imaginary part, they'll eventually move the class to another room and tell everyone else except you.

comics.title: 3D
comics.altText: The LINACs in the glasses frames can barely manage one MeV. You should've gone to the screening at CERN.

comics.title: Stingray Nebula
comics.altText: Eärendil will patrol the walls of night only until the sun reaches red giant stage, engulfing the Morning Star on his brow. Light and high beauty are passing things as well.

comics.title: Dental Nerve
comics.altText: WAKE up in the MORning and my BREATH ain't PREtty / and noBODY'S gonna KISS me if my MOUTH smells SHItty / so I ALways brush my TEETH before I START on the JACK / sure, my DRINKing's out of HAND, but I'm conTROLLing my PLAQUE.

comics.title: Modern History
comics.altText: During the week, I research my character by living in his house and raising his children.

comics.title: Good Code
comics.altText: You can either hang out in the Android Loop or the HURD loop.

comics.title: Misconceptions
comics.altText: 'Grandpa, what was it like in the Before time?' 'It was hell. People went around saying glass was a slow-flowing liquid. You folks these days don't know how good you have it.'

comics.title: Mark
comics.altText: I'm a solipsistic conspiracy theorist. I'm sure I must be up to something, and I will not stop until I find out what.

comics.title: Audiophiles
comics.altText: For years, I took the wrong lesson from that Monster Cable experiment and only listened to my music through alligator-clipped coat hangers.

comics.title: Serious
comics.altText: Not to be confused with Serious PuTTY, the Windows terminal client where everything is in Impact.

comics.title: Explorers
comics.altText: We're going to have to work together to get over our hangups if we're going to learn to move on Catan's hexagonal grid. It's bad enough that we lost our crew of pawns when we passed within firing range of Battleship.

comics.title: Incident
comics.altText: He sees you when you're sleeping, he knows when you're awake, he's copied on /var/spool/mail/root, so be good for goodness' sake.

comics.altText: Not only is that terrible in general, but you just KNOW Billy's going to open the root present first, and then everyone will have to wait while the heap is rebuilt.

comics.title: Convincing
comics.altText: And if you labeled your axes, I could tell you exactly how MUCH better.

comics.title: Tic-Tac-Toe
comics.altText: The only winning move is to play, perfectly, waiting for your opponent to make a mistake.

comics.title: Weather Radar
comics.altText: Ever notice how there aren't as many thunderstorms now as there were when you were a kid?  Much like 'the shuffle on my MP3 player has a bias', this is occasionally true but universally believed. Brains are so interesting!

comics.title: Genetic Analysis
comics.altText: There's still a chance you were conceived via IVF. But we've checked your mom's college yearbook photos, and whether or not she and your father had sex, it's clear that ... listen, I know this is hard for you.

comics.title: Arsenic-Based Life
comics.altText: According to a new paper published in the journal Science, reporters are unable to thrive in an arsenic-rich environment.

comics.title: Positive Attitude
comics.altText: Having a positive attitude is almost tautologically good for your mental health, and extreme stress can hurt your immune system, but that doesn't mean you should feel like shit for feeling like shit.

comics.title: My Business Idea
comics.altText: We didn't believe you at first, but we asked like three people who were at that party. They not only corroborated your story, but even said you totally mentioned wanting to start a company someday. Sorry! If this isn't enough money, let us know.

comics.title: Mutual
comics.altText: A universe that needed someone to observe it in order to collapse it into existence would be a pretty sorry universe indeed.

comics.title: Starlight

comics.title: Connected
comics.altText: Or love in general, for that matter. It just leads to the idea that either your love is pure, perfect, and eternal, and you are storybook-compatible in every way with no problems, or you're LYING when you say 'I love you'.

comics.title: Tech Support
comics.altText: I recently had someone ask me to go get a computer and turn it on so I could restart it. He refused to move further in the script until I said I had done that.

comics.title: Paradise City
comics.altText: Take me down to the paradise municipality / where the grass is mauve and the girls aren't fromthisreality.
